_comment: Vishnu Sahasranāmam — Thousand Names of Viṣṇu from the Mahābhārata (Anuśāsana Parva, Ch. 149), narrated by Bhīṣma to Yudhiṣṭhira. Shemaroo Bhakti's traditional 28:32 rendering. **PHASE B (complete):** Pūrvabhāga framing (lines 0-4), Śāntākāram dhyāna (line 5), all 108 verses of the Sahasranāma proper (lines 6-113, names 1-1000 with traditional Vaiṣṇava meanings), all 19 Phala-śruti verses (lines 114-132), and the closing Samarpaṇa (line 133). Each verse line carries the full canonical Devanagari + IAST + word-by-word meaning, anchored by per-verse timestamps within block-boundaries from the curator's research document. Name meanings follow the classical Pārameśvara-Śaṅkara-Anantakṛṣṇa-Śāstrī tradition; some verses include alternative etymologies (e.g. Avijñātā/Vijñātā in v.51, Sundā/Sundara in v.85).
title: Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149)

lines.devanagari: ॐ शुक्लाम्बरधरं विष्णुं शशिवर्णं चतुर्भुजम् । प्रसन्नवदनं ध्यायेत् सर्वविघ्नोपशान्तये ॥
lines.iast: oṃ śuklāmbaradharaṃ viṣṇuṃ śaśivarṇaṃ caturbhujam | prasannavadanaṃ dhyāyet sarvavighnopaśāntaye ||
lines.english_literal: OM. One should meditate on Viṣṇu — white-robed, moon-pale, four-armed, of serene face — for the calming of all obstacles.
lines.english_poetic: OM. Let us meditate on Viṣṇu — robed in white, moon-pale, four-armed, serene of face — for the stilling of every obstacle.
lines.explanation: Opening obstacle-removing invocation, recited at the start of many Sanskrit liturgies. Sets the meditative tone before the Sahasranāmam proper.

words.meaning: for the calming of all obstacles

lines.devanagari: व्यासं वसिष्ठनप्तारं शक्तेः पौत्रमकल्मषम् । पराशरात्मजं वन्दे शुकतातं तपोनिधिम् ॥
lines.iast: vyāsaṃ vasiṣṭha-naptāraṃ śakteḥ pautram-akalmaṣam | parāśarātmajaṃ vande śuka-tātaṃ tapo-nidhim ||
lines.english_literal: I salute Vyāsa — Vasiṣṭha's great-grandson, Śakti's grandson, the stainless one; Parāśara's son, Śuka's father, treasury of penance.
lines.english_poetic: I bow to Vyāsa — Vasiṣṭha's great-grandson, Śakti's grandson, the stainless one; Parāśara's son, Śuka's father, a treasury of austerity.
lines.explanation: Salutation to Veda Vyāsa, the seer (ṛṣi) of the Sahasranāmam and compiler of the Mahābhārata. The verse names his lineage: Vasiṣṭha → Śakti → Parāśara → Vyāsa → Śuka.

lines.devanagari: यस्य स्मरणमात्रेण जन्मसंसारबन्धनात् । विमुच्यते नमस्तस्मै विष्णवे प्रभविष्णवे ॥ ॐ नमो विष्णवे प्रभविष्णवे ॥
lines.iast: yasya smaraṇa-mātreṇa janma-saṃsāra-bandhanāt | vimucyate namas-tasmai viṣṇave prabhaviṣṇave || oṃ namo viṣṇave prabhaviṣṇave ||
lines.english_literal: By whose mere remembrance one is freed from the bondage of birth-and-saṃsāra — salutations to that Viṣṇu, the all-mighty.
lines.english_poetic: By the mere remembrance of whom a being is set free from the chains of birth and saṃsāra — salutations to that Viṣṇu, the all-mighty Lord.
lines.explanation: Central claim of the entire Sahasranāmam: remembrance (smaraṇa) of Viṣṇu's name is itself the liberating act.

words.meaning: from the bondage of birth and saṃsāra

lines.devanagari: युधिष्ठिर उवाच — किमेकं दैवतं लोके किं वाप्येकं परायणम् । स्तुवन्तः कं कमर्चन्तः प्राप्नुयुर्मानवाः शुभम् ॥ को धर्मः सर्वधर्माणां भवतः परमो मतः । किं जपन्मुच्यते जन्तुर्जन्मसंसारबन्धनात् ॥
lines.iast: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca — kim ekaṃ daivataṃ loke kiṃ vāpyekaṃ parāyaṇam | stuvantaḥ kaṃ kam arcantaḥ prāpnuyur mānavāḥ śubham || ko dharmaḥ sarva-dharmāṇāṃ bhavataḥ paramo mataḥ | kiṃ japan mucyate jantur janma-saṃsāra-bandhanāt ||
lines.english_literal: Yudhiṣṭhira said: Who is the one Godhead, what the one refuge? Praising whom, worshipping whom, do humans attain auspiciousness? Which dharma do you consider supreme? By chanting what does a being become free from birth-and-saṃsāra?
lines.english_poetic: Yudhiṣṭhira asked: Who is the one God of this world? What is the one ultimate refuge? Whom should we praise, whom should we worship, to obtain what is good? Of all the dharmas, which is the highest? And by chanting what does a being escape the chains of birth and rebirth?
lines.explanation: Yudhiṣṭhira's six famous questions to Bhīṣma. After the Kurukṣetra war, the king-philosopher asks the dying patriarch for the most condensed possible spiritual guidance. The entire Sahasranāmam is Bhīṣma's reply.

lines.devanagari: श्रीभीष्म उवाच — जगत्प्रभुं देवदेवमनन्तं पुरुषोत्तमम् । स्तुवन् नामसहस्रेण पुरुषः सततोत्थितः ॥ अनादिनिधनं विष्णुं सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् । लोकाध्यक्षं स्तुवन् नित्यं सर्वदुःखातिगो भवेत् ॥
lines.iast: śrī-bhīṣma uvāca — jagat-prabhuṃ deva-devam anantaṃ puruṣottamam | stuvan nāma-sahasreṇa puruṣaḥ satatotthitaḥ || anādi-nidhanaṃ viṣṇuṃ sarva-loka-maheśvaram | lokādhyakṣaṃ stuvan nityaṃ sarva-duḥkhātigo bhavet ||
lines.english_literal: Bhīṣma said: A person ever-rising, praising the Lord-of-the-world, God-of-gods, the endless Supreme Person, by means of the thousand names. Praising daily Viṣṇu, who is without beginning or end, the great Lord of all worlds, the overseer of the worlds — one would transcend all sorrows.
lines.english_poetic: Bhīṣma replied: A person who daily and steadfastly praises the Lord of the universe, God of gods, the boundless Supreme Person, through the thousand names — praising Viṣṇu without beginning or end, great Lord of all worlds, witness of all — transcends every sorrow.
lines.explanation: Bhīṣma's reply names Viṣṇu through five great titles — Jagat-prabhu, Deva-deva, Ananta, Puruṣottama, Anādi-nidhana — and prescribes daily chanting of the thousand names. The rest of his discourse continues to ~4:30 establishing the Vyāsa-as-ṛṣi tradition, the Anuṣṭup meter, and the ritual context.

words.meaning: by means of the thousand names

lines.devanagari: शान्ताकारं भुजगशयनं पद्मनाभं सुरेशं विश्वाधारं गगनसदृशं मेघवर्णं शुभाङ्गम् । लक्ष्मीकान्तं कमलनयनं योगिभिर्ध्यानगम्यं वन्दे विष्णुं भवभयहरं सर्वलोकैकनाथम् ॥
lines.iast: śāntākāraṃ bhujaga-śayanaṃ padma-nābhaṃ sureśaṃ viśvādhāraṃ gagana-sadṛśaṃ megha-varṇaṃ śubhāṅgam | lakṣmī-kāntaṃ kamala-nayanaṃ yogibhir dhyāna-gamyaṃ vande viṣṇuṃ bhava-bhaya-haraṃ sarva-lokaika-nātham ||
lines.english_literal: Of peaceful form, reclining on the serpent, lotus-naveled, Lord of the gods, support of the universe, sky-like, cloud-coloured, of auspicious limbs; beloved of Lakṣmī, lotus-eyed, attainable by yogis through meditation — I salute Viṣṇu, remover of saṃsāra's fear, sole Lord of all worlds.
lines.english_poetic: He whose form is peace itself, reclining upon the cosmic serpent; lotus-naveled, lord of the celestials; the bearing-up of the universe, vast as the sky, dark as a rain-cloud, of beautiful limbs. Beloved of Lakṣmī, lotus-eyed, found by yogis in meditation — to Him I bow, to Viṣṇu, who removes the fear of saṃsāra, sole Lord of every world.
lines.explanation: The most famous dhyāna-śloka of Viṣṇu — recited at the start of every Vaiṣṇava pūjā. It collects the entire iconography into one verse: śāntākāra, bhujaga-śayana, padma-nābha, megha-varṇa, Lakṣmī-kānta, kamala-nayana.

lines.devanagari: विश्वं विष्णुर्वषट्कारो भूतभव्यभवत्प्रभुः । भूतकृद्भूतभृद्भावो भूतात्मा भूतभावनः ॥
lines.iast: viśvaṃ viṣṇur vaṣaṭkāro bhūta-bhavya-bhavat-prabhuḥ | bhūta-kṛd bhūta-bhṛd bhāvo bhūtātmā bhūta-bhāvanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The universe, Viṣṇu, the sacrificial utterance, Lord of past-future-present; creator of beings, supporter of beings, pure being, soul of beings, originator of beings.
lines.english_poetic: He is the Universe itself, the All-Pervader, the sacred utterance VAṢAṬ; Lord of what was, will be, and is. He creates all beings, sustains them, IS pure being, is the soul of all and the source of all elements.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 1 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: पूतात्मा परमात्मा च मुक्तानां परमा गतिः । अव्ययः पुरुषः साक्षी क्षेत्रज्ञोऽक्षर एव च ॥
lines.iast: pūtātmā paramātmā ca muktānāṃ paramā gatiḥ | avyayaḥ puruṣaḥ sākṣī kṣetrajño'kṣara eva ca ||
lines.english_literal: Pure-Self, Supreme-Self, supreme goal of the liberated; imperishable, the Person, witness, knower-of-the-field, indestructible.
lines.english_poetic: The pure Self, the Supreme Self, the supreme goal of those who are free; the imperishable, the cosmic Person, the silent witness, the knower of the body-field, the indestructible.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 2 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: योगो योगविदां नेता प्रधानपुरुषेश्वरः । नारसिंहवपुः श्रीमान् केशवः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥
lines.iast: yogo yogavidāṃ netā pradhāna-puruṣeśvaraḥ | nārasiṃha-vapuḥ śrīmān keśavaḥ puruṣottamaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Yoga, leader of yoga-knowers, Lord of pradhāna and puruṣa; man-lion-bodied, glorious, Keśava, the Supreme Person.
lines.english_poetic: He is Yoga itself, the leader of those established in yoga, Lord of both Prakṛti and Puruṣa; the man-lion form, the glorious one (Śrīmān), Keśava of beautiful locks, the Supreme Person.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 3 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: सर्वः शर्वः शिवः स्थाणुर्भूतादिर्निधिरव्ययः । सम्भवो भावनो भर्ता प्रभवः प्रभुरीश्वरः ॥
lines.iast: sarvaḥ śarvaḥ śivaḥ sthāṇur bhūtādir nidhir avyayaḥ | sambhavo bhāvano bhartā prabhavaḥ prabhur īśvaraḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The all, the destroyer, the auspicious, the steady-pillar, source-of-elements, indestructible-treasure; manifest-born, generator, supporter, source, master, Lord.
lines.english_poetic: He is All; the destroyer; the auspicious; the immovable pillar; source of elements, the unchanging seed-treasury in which the universe rests at dissolution. He is the self-born, the generator of karmic fruits, the supporter, the source, the master, the supreme Lord.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 4 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: स्वयम्भूः शम्भुरादित्यः पुष्कराक्षो महास्वनः । अनादिनिधनो धाता विधाता धातुरुत्तमः ॥
lines.iast: svayambhūḥ śambhur ādityaḥ puṣkarākṣo mahā-svanaḥ | anādi-nidhano dhātā vidhātā dhātur-uttamaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Self-born, bestower-of-bliss, Āditya, lotus-eyed, of-great-sound; beginningless-endless, the supporter, the disposer, supreme support.
lines.english_poetic: Self-existent, uncaused; bestower of bliss; the golden Āditya in the sun's orb; lotus-eyed; whose great sound is the Veda. Without beginning or end, He supports, regulates, and is the ultimate ground of all that is.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 5 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: अप्रमेयो हृषीकेशः पद्मनाभोऽमरप्रभुः । विश्वकर्मा मनुस्त्वष्टा स्थविष्ठः स्थविरो ध्रुवः ॥
lines.iast: aprameyo hṛṣīkeśaḥ padmanābho 'mara-prabhuḥ | viśva-karmā manus tvaṣṭā sthaviṣṭhaḥ sthaviro dhruvaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Unmeasurable, master-of-senses, lotus-naveled, Lord-of-deathless-ones; world-maker, the thinker, the shaper, the immense, the most ancient and firm.
lines.english_poetic: Beyond all measure, master of the senses, lotus-naveled, Lord of the deathless gods. World-maker whose work is wondrous; He thinks the world (Manu), He shapes it (Tvaṣṭā). Immense, most ancient, eternally firm.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 6 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: अग्राह्यः शाश्वतः कृष्णो लोहिताक्षः प्रतर्दनः । प्रभूतस्त्रिककुब्धाम पवित्रं मङ्गलं परम् ॥
lines.iast: agrāhyaḥ śāśvataḥ kṛṣṇo lohitākṣaḥ pratardanaḥ | prabhūtas tri-kakub-dhāma pavitraṃ maṅgalaṃ param ||
lines.english_literal: Ungraspable, eternal, Kṛṣṇa, red-eyed, the destroyer; great, support-of-three-quarters, purifier, supremely auspicious.
lines.english_poetic: He cannot be grasped by sense or mind; eternal, He is Kṛṣṇa (existence-knowledge-bliss); His eyes are tinged red; He destroys all at dissolution. He is great, support of all three regions, the purifier, supreme auspiciousness itself.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 7 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: ईशानः प्राणदः प्राणो ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः प्रजापतिः । हिरण्यगर्भो भूगर्भो माधवो मधुसूदनः ॥
lines.iast: īśānaḥ prāṇadaḥ prāṇo jyeṣṭhaḥ śreṣṭhaḥ prajāpatiḥ | hiraṇya-garbho bhū-garbho mādhavo madhusūdanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Controller, life-giver, life-itself, eldest, most-praiseworthy, lord-of-creatures; golden-wombed, earth-wombed, Mādhava, killer-of-Madhu.
lines.english_poetic: He controls all; gives prāṇa, IS prāṇa; the eldest, the highest, lord of creatures; the golden-wombed (Hiraṇyagarbha, the cosmic seed); the earth-wombed; Mādhava (consort of Mā / Lakṣmī); slayer of the demon Madhu.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 8 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: ईश्वरो विक्रमी धन्वी मेधावी विक्रमः क्रमः । अनुत्तमो दुराधर्षः कृतज्ञः कृतिरात्मवान् ॥
lines.iast: īśvaro vikramī dhanvī medhāvī vikramaḥ kramaḥ | anuttamo durādharṣaḥ kṛtajñaḥ kṛtir ātmavān ||
lines.english_literal: Lord, valorous, bow-armed, wise, the stride, the order; unsurpassed, unassailable, knower-of-the-done, the achievement, self-possessed.
lines.english_poetic: Almighty Lord, valorous, armed with the bow; of great intelligence; the stride (Vāmana's) and the order (the cosmic sequence); unsurpassed; unassailable by demons; knower of what every soul has done; the achievement itself; established in His own greatness.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 9 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [82] knower of all karma, pleased by simple offerings

lines.devanagari: सुरेशः शरणं शर्म विश्वरेताः प्रजाभवः । अहः संवत्सरो व्यालः प्रत्ययः सर्वदर्शनः ॥
lines.iast: sureśaḥ śaraṇaṃ śarma viśva-retāḥ prajā-bhavaḥ | ahaḥ saṃvatsaro vyālaḥ pratyayaḥ sarva-darśanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Lord-of-gods, refuge, bliss, world-seed, source-of-beings; day, year, the serpent, the witness, the all-seeing.
lines.english_poetic: Lord of all gods, the sole refuge, bliss itself; the cosmic seed, source of all beings. He is the Day, the cosmic Year, the elusive serpent (Vyāla = Ananta), the inner witness, the all-seer.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 10 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [87] of the nature of supreme bliss
[93] of the nature of pure consciousness

lines.devanagari: अजः सर्वेश्वरः सिद्धः सिद्धिः सर्वादिरच्युतः । वृषाकपिरमेयात्मा सर्वयोगविनिःसृतः ॥
lines.iast: ajaḥ sarveśvaraḥ siddhaḥ siddhiḥ sarvādir acyutaḥ | vṛṣākapir ameyātmā sarva-yoga-viniḥsṛtaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Unborn, Lord-of-all, perfected, perfection, source-of-all, infallible; the shower-of-desires, immeasurable Self, untouched-by-bondage.
lines.english_poetic: The unborn, Lord of all, perfected one, perfection itself; first cause of everything, the unfallen (Acyuta); Vṛṣākapi the shower of desires; whose Self cannot be measured; standing aside from every bondage.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 11 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [97] ever established in his own nature

lines.devanagari: वसुर्वसुमनाः सत्यः समात्माऽसम्मितः समः । अमोघः पुण्डरीकाक्षो वृषकर्मा वृषाकृतिः ॥
lines.iast: vasur vasumanāḥ satyaḥ samātmā 'sammitaḥ samaḥ | amoghaḥ puṇḍarīkākṣo vṛṣa-karmā vṛṣākṛtiḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The wealth, great-minded, truth, even-souled, immeasurable, equal; unfailing, lotus-eyed, dharma-acting, dharma-formed.
lines.english_poetic: He is the wealth in which all dwell; the great-minded; truth itself; even-souled and impartial to all; immeasurable; equanimous. Unfailing in worship, lotus-eyed; whose every act is dharma; whose very form is dharma.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 12 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [105] of great mind free of attachments
[110] worship of whom never goes in vain

lines.devanagari: रुद्रो बहुशिरा बभ्रुर्विश्वयोनिः शुचिश्रवाः । अमृतः शाश्वतस्थाणुर्वरारोहो महातपाः ॥
lines.iast: rudro bahu-śirā babhrur viśva-yoniḥ śuci-śravāḥ | amṛtaḥ śāśvata-sthāṇur varāroho mahā-tapāḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Rudra, many-headed, the governor, world-source, of-pure-fame; deathless, eternally-firm, of-noble-ascent, great-austerity.
lines.english_poetic: Rudra, who makes all beings cry at dissolution; many-headed; governor of worlds; source of the universe; whose names and glories are purifying. Deathless, eternally firm, His lap is the highest blessing — His austerity (the knowledge-fire of creation) is immense.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 13 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [114] who makes beings cry at dissolution
[121] whose lap is the highest blessing

lines.devanagari: सर्वगः सर्वविद्भानुर्विष्वक्सेनो जनार्दनः । वेदो वेदविदव्यङ्गो वेदाङ्गो वेदवित् कविः ॥
lines.iast: sarva-gaḥ sarva-vid bhānur viṣvak-seno janārdanaḥ | vedo veda-vid avyaṅgo vedāṅgo veda-vit kaviḥ ||
lines.english_literal: All-pervading, all-knowing, the radiant, world-scattering-the-foe, afflicter-of-the-wicked; the Veda, knower-of-Veda, unblemished, Veda-limbed, Veda-knower, the seer.
lines.english_poetic: He pervades everything; knows all; radiant; before His army the Asuras scatter; He afflicts the wicked. He IS the Veda, the knower of the Veda, without any defect; the Vedas are his limbs; He knows them all; He is the cosmic seer.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 14 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [127] of the form of the Veda
[128] knower of Veda and its meaning
[130] to whom the Vedas are limbs

lines.devanagari: लोकाध्यक्षः सुराध्यक्षो धर्माध्यक्षः कृताकृतः । चतुरात्मा चतुर्व्यूहश्चतुर्दंष्ट्रश्चतुर्भुजः ॥
lines.iast: lokādhyakṣaḥ surādhyakṣo dharmādhyakṣaḥ kṛtākṛtaḥ | catur-ātmā catur-vyūhaś catur-daṃṣṭraś catur-bhujaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: World-witness, god-witness, dharma-witness, effect-and-non-effect; four-souled, four-arrayed, four-fanged, four-armed.
lines.english_poetic: Witness of all the worlds; overlord of the gods; witness of dharma and adharma; both effect (the world) and non-effect (its cause). Fourfold as Self, as cosmic array (Vyūha), four-fanged (as Narasiṃha), four-armed.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 15 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: भ्राजिष्णुर्भोजनं भोक्ता सहिष्णुर्जगदादिजः । अनघो विजयो जेता विश्वयोनिः पुनर्वसुः ॥
lines.iast: bhrājiṣṇur bhojanaṃ bhoktā sahiṣṇur jagad-ādi-jaḥ | anagho vijayo jetā viśva-yoniḥ punar-vasuḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Pure-luminosity, the enjoyed, enjoyer, forbearing, world-first-born; sinless, victorious, conqueror, world-source, dweller-again.
lines.english_poetic: Pure luminosity; the enjoyed (prakṛti) and the enjoyer (puruṣa); forbearing; first-born (as Hiraṇyagarbha); sinless; victorious by His six glories; naturally superior to all; source of the universe; dweller in every body as the jīva.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 16 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: उपेन्द्रो वामनः प्रांशुरमोघः शुचिरूर्जितः । अतीन्द्रः सङ्ग्रहः सर्गो धृतात्मा नियमो यमः ॥
lines.iast: upendro vāmanaḥ prāṃśur amoghaḥ śucir ūrjitaḥ | atīndraḥ saṅgrahaḥ sargo dhṛtātmā niyamo yamaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Younger-Indra, the dwarf, of-great-height, unfailing, pure, of-infinite-strength; superior-to-Indra, the gather, creation, self-poised, ordainer, regulator.
lines.english_poetic: Born as younger brother to Indra (Upendra); the Vāmana who in three strides covered the worlds; of immense stature; whose acts never fail; the purifier; of infinite strength; superior to Indra; the cosmic gathering; the act of creation; ever in His own nature; appointer of each being to its station; regulator from within.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 17 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [154] whose acts never go in vain
[160] ever in His own form, beyond birth-death

lines.devanagari: वेद्यो वैद्यः सदायोगी वीरहा माधवो मधुः । अतीन्द्रियो महामायो महोत्साहो महाबलः ॥
lines.iast: vedyo vaidyaḥ sadā-yogī vīra-hā mādhavo madhuḥ | atīndriyo mahā-māyo mahotsāho mahā-balaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The knowable, the all-knower, ever-yogi, hero-slayer, Mādhava, sweetness; beyond-senses, great-magician, of-great-endeavour, of-great-strength.
lines.english_poetic: He who must be known (by mokṣa-seekers); the knower of all branches of knowledge; ever-in-yoga; slayer of demon-heroes; Mādhava the Lord of knowledge; sweet as honey to His devotees. Beyond the senses; greater than the great illusionists; ever active in creation-sustenance-dissolution; the strongest of the strong.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 18 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [164] knower of all branches of knowledge

lines.devanagari: महाबुद्धिर्महावीर्यो महाशक्तिर्महाद्युतिः । अनिर्देश्यवपुः श्रीमानमेयात्मा महाद्रिधृक् ॥
lines.iast: mahā-buddhir mahā-vīryo mahā-śaktir mahā-dyutiḥ | anirdeśya-vapuḥ śrīmān ameyātmā mahādri-dhṛk ||
lines.english_literal: Of-great-wisdom, of-great-virility, of-great-power, of-great-brilliance; of-indescribable-form, glorious, immeasurable-Self, holder-of-the-great-mountain.
lines.english_poetic: Wisest of the wise; most virile; most powerful (His Māyā being saṃsāra-causing); most brilliant within and without. His form cannot be pointed out ("He is this") for He is beyond objectivity. Glorious in every way; immeasurable in Self; holder of the great Mount Mandara (at the churning) and Govardhana (as Kṛṣṇa).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 19 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: महेष्वासो महीभर्ता श्रीनिवासः सतां गतिः । अनिरुद्धः सुरानन्दो गोविन्दो गोविदां पतिः ॥
lines.iast: maheṣvāso mahī-bhartā śrī-nivāsaḥ satāṃ gatiḥ | aniruddhaḥ surānando govindo govidāṃ patiḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Great-bowman, earth-bearer, abode-of-Śrī, refuge-of-the-good; unobstructed, joy-of-gods, Govinda, master-of-word-knowers.
lines.english_poetic: Wielder of the great bow; upholder of the earth in pralaya-waters; on whose chest Śrī (Lakṣmī) eternally dwells; highest destiny of holy ones. Never obstructed in any manifestation; joy of all divinities; Govinda who gives power to all words; master of those who know words.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 20 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [187] who pervades all words, giving them power
[188] master of those who know words

lines.devanagari: मरीचिर्दमनो हंसः सुपर्णो भुजगोत्तमः । हिरण्यनाभः सुतपाः पद्मनाभः प्रजापतिः ॥
lines.iast: marīcir damano haṃsaḥ suparṇo bhujagottamaḥ | hiraṇya-nābhaḥ sutapāḥ padma-nābhaḥ prajāpatiḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The ray, restrainer, the swan, the well-winged, best-of-serpents; golden-naveled, of-great-austerity, lotus-naveled, lord-of-creatures.
lines.english_poetic: The supreme power seen in glorious beings; restrainer of the wicked (as Yama); the swan who removes saṃsāra-fear; well-winged (dharma and adharma as wings); best of serpents (Ananta is His power). Golden-naveled (from whose navel Brahmā arose); great ascetic (as Nara-Nārāyaṇa at Badarī); lotus-naveled; father of all.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 21 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [194] from whose golden navel Brahmā arose

lines.devanagari: अमृत्युः सर्वदृक् सिंहः सन्धाता सन्धिमान् स्थिरः । अजो दुर्मर्षणः शास्ता विश्रुतात्मा सुरारिहा ॥
lines.iast: amṛtyuḥ sarva-dṛk siṃhaḥ sandhātā sandhimān sthiraḥ | ajo durmarṣaṇaḥ śāstā viśrutātmā surāri-hā ||
lines.english_literal: Deathless, all-seeing, the lion, uniter, the joined, firm; unborn, unbearable, teacher, well-known-Self, slayer-of-divine-foes.
lines.english_poetic: Without death or its cause; sees the karmas of all; the lion (Narasiṃha) who destroys; uniter of beings with karmic fruit; Himself the enjoyer of those fruits; ever firm. Unborn (or: who enters devotees' hearts); whose might Asuras cannot bear; teacher through scriptures; known through 'Truth' and 'Knowledge'; slayer of the gods' enemies.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 22 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [201] uniter of beings with karmic fruit
[208] slayer of enemies of the gods

lines.devanagari: गुरुर्गुरुतमो धाम सत्यः सत्यपराक्रमः । निमिषोऽनिमिषः स्रग्वी वाचस्पतिरुदारधीः ॥
lines.iast: gurur gurutamo dhāma satyaḥ satya-parākramaḥ | nimiṣo 'nimiṣaḥ sragvī vācas-patir udāra-dhīḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Teacher, supreme-teacher, abode, truth, of-true-valor; the-blinking, unblinking, garlanded, lord-of-speech, of-broad-mind.
lines.english_poetic: The greatest teacher; teacher of all forms of knowledge; the Supreme Light (dhāma); embodied as Truth; of unfailing valour. Sometimes in yogic sleep (eyes closed); ever awake; wearing the Vaijayantī garland (woven of the subtle five elements); Lord of speech and of broad-intellect — both names together.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 23 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [217] Lord of speech and broad intellect

lines.devanagari: अग्रणीर्ग्रामणीः श्रीमान् न्यायो नेता समीरणः । सहस्रमूर्धा विश्वात्मा सहस्राक्षः सहस्रपात् ॥
lines.iast: agraṇīr grāmaṇīḥ śrīmān nyāyo netā samīraṇaḥ | sahasra-mūrdhā viśvātmā sahasrākṣaḥ sahasra-pāt ||
lines.english_literal: Forefront, host-leader, glorious, justice, leader, the breather; thousand-headed, world-Self, thousand-eyed, thousand-footed.
lines.english_poetic: Who leads mokṣa-seekers to the highest; commander of the host of beings; resplendent above all; the principle of consistency (nyāya) that points to non-duality; mover of the becoming-world; as breath, He keeps living beings alive. Echoing the Puruṣa Sūkta: thousand-headed, soul of the universe, thousand-eyed, thousand-footed.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 24 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [218] who leads to the highest status

lines.devanagari: आवर्तनो निवृत्तात्मा संवृतः सम्प्रमर्दनः । अहः संवर्तको वह्निरनिलो धरणीधरः ॥
lines.iast: āvartano nivṛttātmā saṃvṛtaḥ sampramardanaḥ | ahaḥ saṃvartako vahnir anilo dharaṇī-dharaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Whirler, the-withdrawn-Self, concealed, great-crusher; the-rotator-of-day, fire, wind, earth-bearer.
lines.english_poetic: Whirler of the wheel of saṃsāra; whose Self is free of saṃsāric bondage; covered by all-covering avidyā; the great crusher (through His Vibhūtis Rudra, Yama, etc.). As the sun, He turns day to night; as fire He carries offerings; as wind He moves; He upholds the worlds, the elephants of the quarters, Ādiśeṣa.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 25 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: सुप्रसादः प्रसन्नात्मा विश्वधृग्विश्वभुग्विभुः । सत्कर्ता सत्कृतः साधुर्जह्नुर्नारायणो नरः ॥
lines.iast: suprasādaḥ prasannātmā viśva-dhṛg viśva-bhug vibhuḥ | sat-kartā sat-kṛtaḥ sādhur jahnur nārāyaṇo naraḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Of-great-grace, serene-Self, world-bearer, world-enjoyer, all-pervading; honourer, honoured, righteous, the absorber, Nārāyaṇa, the man.
lines.english_poetic: Of uniquely wonderful grace (giving mokṣa even to Śiśupāla who hated Him); serene-Self never contaminated by rajas-tamas; bearer and enjoyer of all worlds; the all-pervading. He honours the good; is honoured even by those who deserve adoration; the righteous; the absorber-of-all at dissolution; Nārāyaṇa (whose abode is in all beings); the eternal Person.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 26 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [242] adored by those who deserve adoration
[245] whose abode is in all beings

lines.devanagari: असङ्ख्येयोऽप्रमेयात्मा विशिष्टः शिष्टकृच्छुचिः । सिद्धार्थः सिद्धसङ्कल्पः सिद्धिदः सिद्धिसाधनः ॥
lines.iast: asaṅkhyeyo 'prameyātmā viśiṣṭaḥ śiṣṭa-kṛc chuciḥ | siddhārthaḥ siddha-saṅkalpaḥ siddhi-daḥ siddhi-sādhanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Numberless, immeasurable-Self, distinguished, protector-of-the-wise, pure; aim-fulfilled, of-fulfilled-resolve, giver-of-fulfilment, the-means-of-fulfilment.
lines.english_poetic: Without count of name and form; whose Self is beyond all means of knowing; distinguished above all; commander of all / protector of the wise; pure. His objects always fulfilled; resolves always fulfilled; giver of siddhi to all who practice; the very means by which fulfilment comes.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 27 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [247] without distinctions of name and form

lines.devanagari: वृषाही वृषभो विष्णुर्वृषपर्वा वृषोदरः । वर्धनो वर्धमानश्च विविक्तः श्रुतिसागरः ॥
lines.iast: vṛṣāhī vṛṣabho viṣṇur vṛṣaparvā vṛṣodaraḥ | vardhano vardhamānaś ca viviktaḥ śruti-sāgaraḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Bull-of-days (= dharma), the-bull, Viṣṇu, bull-jointed, bull-bellied; the-increaser, ever-growing, the-distinct, ocean-of-scripture.
lines.english_poetic: He who is dharma (vṛṣa); the cosmic bull; Viṣṇu; whose joints (parvas) are the steps of dharma; whose belly showers offspring (devotees). Increaser of the devotee's ecstasy; ever-growing as the universe; the distinct, untouched; ocean from which the śruti flows.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 28 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [259] whose joints are steps of dharma

lines.devanagari: सुभुजो दुर्धरो वाग्मी महेन्द्रो वसुदो वसुः । नैकरूपो बृहद्रूपः शिपिविष्टः प्रकाशनः ॥
lines.iast: subhujo durdharo vāgmī mahendro vasu-do vasuḥ | naika-rūpo bṛhad-rūpaḥ śipi-viṣṭaḥ prakāśanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Beautiful-armed, hard-to-hold, eloquent, great-Indra, wealth-giver, the-treasure; many-formed, of-vast-form, dwelling-in-rays, the-illuminator.
lines.english_poetic: Beautiful-armed who protects worlds; hard for any to hold up the universe as He does; from whom the Vedic words come; the great Lord (Mahendra); giver of wealth; Himself the Vasu. Not of any single form; of mysterious vast forms (like the Boar); dwelling in cows / in solar rays as Yajña; illuminator of everything.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 29 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [266] who alone holds up the universe

lines.devanagari: ओजस्तेजोद्युतिधरः प्रकाशात्मा प्रतापनः । ऋद्धः स्पष्टाक्षरो मन्त्रश्चन्द्रांशुर्भास्करद्युतिः ॥
lines.iast: ojas-tejo-dyuti-dharaḥ prakāśātmā pratāpanaḥ | ṛddhaḥ spaṣṭākṣaro mantraś candrāṃśur bhāskara-dyutiḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Holder-of-vitality-vigor-brilliance, of-radiant-form, the-warmer; the-prosperous, of-clear-syllable, the-mantra, moonbeam, sun-radiance.
lines.english_poetic: Endowed with vitality, vigor, and brilliance; whose form is radiance itself; who warms all worlds (as the sun). Rich in dharma, jñāna, vairāgya; whose 'clear syllable' is Oṃ; who is the Mantra itself / known by Mantras. His moonbeams cool saṃsāric burning; His solar effulgence reveals all.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 30 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [277] who warms the worlds as sun

lines.devanagari: अमृतांशूद्भवो भानुः शशबिन्दुः सुरेश्वरः । औषधं जगतः सेतुः सत्यधर्मपराक्रमः ॥
lines.iast: amṛtāṃśūdbhavo bhānuḥ śaśa-binduḥ sureśvaraḥ | auṣadhaṃ jagataḥ setuḥ satya-dharma-parākramaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Source-of-the-moon, the-shining, hare-marked, Lord-of-gods; medicine, world-bridge, of-true-dharma-and-valor.
lines.english_poetic: From whom the moon arose at the churning; the radiant; the moon ("hare-marked"); Lord of gods and the good. The medicine for the saṃsāric disease; the bridge across the ocean of becoming; whose dharma, knowledge, valour are all true.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 31 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [286] Lord of gods and the good
[288] bridge across the ocean of becoming

lines.devanagari: भूतभव्यभवन्नाथः पवनः पावनोऽनलः । कामहा कामकृत्कान्तः कामः कामप्रदः प्रभुः ॥
lines.iast: bhūta-bhavya-bhavan-nāthaḥ pavanaḥ pāvano 'nalaḥ | kāma-hā kāma-kṛt kāntaḥ kāmaḥ kāma-pradaḥ prabhuḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Lord-of-past-future-present, purifier, mover, fire-of-life; desire-destroyer, desire-fulfiller, beloved, desire, desire-bestower, master.
lines.english_poetic: Master of all beings of all three times; the purifier; the mover; the jīva's fire (anala = prāṇa). He destroys desire in mokṣa-seekers; fulfils desire in pure devotees; the supremely beautiful; the desired one; bestower of all four life-goals; surpassing all.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 32 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [290] Lord of past, present, future beings

lines.devanagari: युगादिकृद्युगावर्तो नैकमायो महाशनः । अदृश्यो व्यक्तरूपश्च सहस्रजिदनन्तजित् ॥
lines.iast: yugādi-kṛd yugāvarto naika-māyo mahā-śanaḥ | adṛśyo vyakta-rūpaś ca sahasra-jid ananta-jit ||
lines.english_literal: Cause-of-yugas, yuga-revolver, of-many-illusions, the-devourer; unseen, manifest-form, thousand-victor, infinite-victor.
lines.english_poetic: Cause of the units of time; in form of time He repeats the four yugas; assumer of countless Māyā-forms; devourer of all at the end of a Kalpa. Unperceivable by the five senses; yet manifest as the universe; victorious over a thousand foes; victorious over the infinite.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 33 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [303] devourer at the end of a Kalpa

lines.devanagari: इष्टोऽविशिष्टः शिष्टेष्टः शिखण्डी नहुषो वृषः । क्रोधहा क्रोधकृत्कर्ता विश्वबाहुर्महीधरः ॥
lines.iast: iṣṭo 'viśiṣṭaḥ śiṣṭeṣṭaḥ śikhaṇḍī nahuṣo vṛṣaḥ | krodha-hā krodha-kṛt kartā viśva-bāhur mahī-dharaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Dear, equal-to-all-and-distinct, dear-to-the-wise, peacock-crowned, the-binder, the-bull; anger-destroyer, anger-causer, doer, all-armed, earth-supporter.
lines.english_poetic: Dear to all (being bliss itself); resides within all (no distinction); dear to the wise; with peacock-feather crown (as Gopāla); the binder by Māyā (Nahuṣa); the embodied dharma (vṛṣa). He eradicates anger in the virtuous and generates anger in the wicked; the doer; all-armed (the universe being His arms); supporter of the earth.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 34 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [314] destroyer of anger in the virtuous
[317] supporter of earth, receiver of worship

lines.devanagari: अच्युतः प्रथितः प्राणः प्राणदो वासवानुजः । अपांनिधिरधिष्ठानमप्रमत्तः प्रतिष्ठितः ॥
lines.iast: acyutaḥ prathitaḥ prāṇaḥ prāṇa-do vāsavānujaḥ | apāṃ-nidhir adhiṣṭhānam apramattaḥ pratiṣṭhitaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Infallible, famed, life, life-giver, younger-of-Indra; ocean, seat, ever-vigilant, well-established.
lines.english_poetic: Without the six transformations of body; famed by His acts; as Hiraṇyagarbha He gives prāṇa to all; bestower-and-withdrawer of strength; younger brother of Vāsava (Indra) as Vāmana. The ocean (collectivity of waters); the seat of everything; ever vigilant in giving karma-fruits; established in His own greatness.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 35 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: स्कन्दः स्कन्दधरो धुर्यो वरदो वायुवाहनः । वासुदेवो बृहद्भानुरादिदेवः पुरन्दरः ॥
lines.iast: skandaḥ skanda-dharo dhuryo varado vāyu-vāhanaḥ | vāsudevo bṛhad-bhānur ādi-devaḥ purandaraḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Mover-of-all-as-air, supporter-of-the-righteous-path, burden-bearer, boon-giver, air-mover; Vāsudeva, great-brilliance, primordial-god, citadel-destroyer.
lines.english_poetic: Who moves all things like air; supporter of the righteous path (Skanda = dharma); bearer of the world's burden; bestower of boons; vibrator of the seven atmospheres. Vāsudeva who is both Vasu and Deva; great in brilliance; the primordial God; destroyer of the cities of foes.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 36 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [332] who is both Vasu and Deva

lines.devanagari: अशोकस्तारणस्तारः शूरः शौरिर्जनेश्वरः । अनुकूलः शतावर्तः पद्मी पद्मनिभेक्षणः ॥
lines.iast: aśokas tāraṇas tāraḥ śūraḥ śaurir janeśvaraḥ | anukūlaḥ śatāvartaḥ padmī padma-nibhekṣaṇaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Sorrow-less, the-uplifter, the-liberator, hero, son-of-Sura, lord-of-beings; favorable, of-hundred-incarnations, lotus-handed, lotus-eyed.
lines.english_poetic: Without the six defects (sorrow, delusion, hunger, thirst, birth, death); uplifter of beings from saṃsāra; liberator from womb, birth, age, death; hero who grants the four puruṣārthas; born as Kṛṣṇa of Vasudeva (Śauri); Lord of all beings. Favourable to all (being their Self); of a hundred-and-more incarnations; lotus-handed; lotus-eyed.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 37 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [342] favourable to all, being their Self

lines.devanagari: पद्मनाभोऽरविन्दाक्षः पद्मगर्भः शरीरभृत् । महर्द्धिरृद्धो वृद्धात्मा महाक्षो गरुडध्वजः ॥
lines.iast: padma-nābho 'ravindākṣaḥ padma-garbhaḥ śarīra-bhṛt | mahardhir ṛddho vṛddhātmā mahākṣo garuḍa-dhvajaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Lotus-naveled, lotus-eyed, lotus-wombed, body-bearer; of-great-prosperity, prospered, ancient-Self, large-eyed, Garuḍa-bannered.
lines.english_poetic: Residing at the centre of the heart-lotus; lotus-eyed; worshipped in the heart-lotus; supporter of beings as food and prāṇa. Of enormous prosperity; standing as the world; whose Self is ancient; with two/many glorious eyes; whose banner bears Garuḍa.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 38 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: अतुलः शरभो भीमः समयज्ञो हविर्हरिः । सर्वलक्षणलक्षण्यो लक्ष्मीवान् समितिञ्जयः ॥
lines.iast: atulaḥ śarabho bhīmaḥ samaya-jño havir-hariḥ | sarva-lakṣaṇa-lakṣaṇyo lakṣmīvān samitiñ-jayaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Incomparable, the-Śarabha, the-awesome, knower-of-the-occasion, taker-of-oblation; marked-by-every-mark, possessor-of-Lakṣmī, victor-in-battle.
lines.english_poetic: Beyond compare; the perishable body (Śarabha) yet beyond it; the awesome (feared by all); knower of the right time for creation, sustenance, dissolution; receiver of the oblation. Known by every criterion of knowledge (the supreme knowledge); the possessor of Lakṣmī on His chest; victor in every battle.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 39 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [358] knower of right time for cosmic acts
[360] supreme knowledge known by every criterion
[361] possessor of Lakṣmī on His chest

lines.devanagari: विक्षरो रोहितो मार्गो हेतुर्दामोदरः सहः । महीधरो महाभागो वेगवानमिताशनः ॥
lines.iast: vikṣaro rohito mārgo hetur dāmodaraḥ sahaḥ | mahī-dharo mahā-bhāgo vegavān amitāśanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Indestructible, the-red-fish, the-path, cause, rope-bellied, subordinator; earth-supporter, fortunate, swift, infinite-consumer.
lines.english_poetic: Without destruction; who became the great fish Rohita; the path sought by mokṣa-seekers; both instrumental and material cause; bellied with mother Yaśodā's rope (Dāmodara — Kṛṣṇa's pastime); subordinator of all. Earth-supporter (in mountain forms); enjoyer of supreme felicities by His own will; of tremendous speed; consumer of all worlds at dissolution.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 40 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [372] consumer of all worlds at dissolution

lines.devanagari: उद्भवः क्षोभणो देवः श्रीगर्भः परमेश्वरः । करणं कारणं कर्ता विकर्ता गहनो गुहः ॥
lines.iast: udbhavaḥ kṣobhaṇo devaḥ śrī-garbhaḥ parameśvaraḥ | karaṇaṃ kāraṇaṃ kartā vikartā gahano guhaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Origin, the-agitator, the-god, Śrī-wombed, supreme-Lord; instrument, cause, doer, world-maker, unfathomable, the-hidden.
lines.english_poetic: Origin (material cause); the agitator who at creation entered Puruṣa and Prakṛti; the divine sporter; in whose abdomen Śrī (His samsāra-manifestation) abides; supreme Lord. The instrument, the cause, the doer; maker of this unique universe; unfathomable in greatness; who hides Himself by Māyā.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 41 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [378] the most important factor in generation

lines.devanagari: व्यवसायो व्यवस्थानः संस्थानः स्थानदो ध्रुवः । परर्द्धिः परमस्पष्टस्तुष्टः पुष्टः शुभेक्षणः ॥
lines.iast: vyavasāyo vyavasthānaḥ saṃsthānaḥ sthāna-do dhruvaḥ | parardhiḥ parama-spaṣṭas tuṣṭaḥ puṣṭaḥ śubhekṣaṇaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Determination, the-order, the-final-rest, place-giver, firm; supreme-prosperity, most-clear, satisfied, well-nourished, of-auspicious-glance.
lines.english_poetic: Pure determination (knowledge itself); the orderly regulator of the universe; the final ground at dissolution; bestower of stations like Dhruva's; indestructibly firm. Of supreme lordliness; in whom 'paramā' Lakṣmī dwells; ever satisfied; nourishing all; whose glance bestows liberation and removes doubt.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 42 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: रामो विरामो विरजो मार्गो नेयो नयोऽनयः । वीरः शक्तिमतां श्रेष्ठो धर्मो धर्मविदुत्तमः ॥
lines.iast: rāmo virāmo virajo mārgo neyo nayo 'nayaḥ | vīraḥ śaktimatāṃ śreṣṭho dharmo dharma-vid uttamaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Rāma, end-of-all, dust-free, the-path, the-led, the-leader, leaderless; hero, best-of-the-powerful, dharma, supreme-dharma-knower.
lines.english_poetic: The eternally blissful in whom yogis delight; the dissolution-end of all; without dust (free of all desire); the path; the one who is led (Jīva) and the leader (Īśvara) and the unled (Supreme). Hero; greatest of the powerful (above Brahmā etc.); dharma itself; the supreme knower of dharma.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 43 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: वैकुण्ठः पुरुषः प्राणः प्राणदः प्रणवः पृथुः । हिरण्यगर्भः शत्रुघ्नो व्याप्तो वायुरधोक्षजः ॥
lines.iast: vaikuṇṭhaḥ puruṣaḥ prāṇaḥ prāṇa-daḥ praṇavaḥ pṛthuḥ | hiraṇya-garbhaḥ śatru-ghno vyāpto vāyur adhokṣajaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Vaikuṇṭha, the-Person, life, life-giver, Praṇava, the-vast; golden-wombed, foe-slayer, pervading, wind, undegrading.
lines.english_poetic: Bringer-together of all diversified categories (Vaikuṇṭha); the cosmic Person who existed before all; the Prāṇa within (kṣetra-jña); giver of life; whom Oṃ-uttering devotees praise; expanded as the world. Cause of Brahmā's golden-egg birth; destroyer of gods' enemies; pervading every effect; who moves toward His devotees; never degrading from His own nature (Adhokṣaja).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 44 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: ऋतुः सुदर्शनः कालः परमेष्ठी परिग्रहः । उग्रः संवत्सरो दक्षो विश्रामो विश्वदक्षिणः ॥
lines.iast: ṛtuḥ sudarśanaḥ kālaḥ parameṣṭhī parigrahaḥ | ugraḥ saṃvatsaro dakṣo viśrāmo viśva-dakṣiṇaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Season, of-good-vision, time, supreme-resident, the-receiver; the-formidable, year, dexterous, the-rest, world-skilful.
lines.english_poetic: Of the nature of time, indicated by the seasons (ṛtu); whose darśana bestows mokṣa (Sudarśana); time-measure itself; dwelling in His supreme greatness in the sky of the heart; grasper-and-receiver of devotees' offerings. Formidable cause of fear even to the sun; the cosmic Year; augmenter as the world; bestower of rest from saṃsāra; most skilful in everything.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 45 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [421] formidable, cause of fear even to sun

lines.devanagari: विस्तारः स्थावरस्थाणुः प्रमाणं बीजमव्ययम् । अर्थोऽनर्थो महाकोशो महाभोगो महाधनः ॥
lines.iast: vistāraḥ sthāvaraḥ-sthāṇuḥ pramāṇaṃ bījam avyayam | artho 'nartho mahā-kośo mahā-bhogo mahā-dhanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Expansion, fixed-pillar, measure, seed-imperishable; goal, non-goal, great-treasury, great-enjoyment, of-great-wealth.
lines.english_poetic: Expanded as all worlds; firm pillar in which firm things like earth rest; pure consciousness (the measure); imperishable seed of saṃsāra. Sought by all as bliss (artha); without any further goal himself (anartha); covered by the great kośas (food-prāṇa-mind-etc.); bliss as the great enjoyment; the entire universe as His wealth.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 46 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: अनिर्विण्णः स्थविष्ठोऽभूर्धर्मयूपो महामखः । नक्षत्रनेमिर्नक्षत्री क्षमः क्षामः समीहनः ॥
lines.iast: anirviṇṇaḥ sthaviṣṭho 'bhūr dharma-yūpo mahā-makhaḥ | nakṣatra-nemir nakṣatrī kṣamaḥ kṣāmaḥ samīhanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Never-heedless, of-huge-form, birthless, dharma-post, great-sacrifice; star-rim, the-moon, capable, beyond-modifications, the-exertor.
lines.english_poetic: Never heedless (ever self-fulfilled); of cosmic huge form (Virāṭ); without birth; sacrificial post to which all dharma-offerings are tied; the great Yajña that yields Nirvāṇa. Heart-rim of the nakṣatras; the moon Himself; clever in everything; pure-Self beyond mind's modifications; great exerter for creation.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 47 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: यज्ञ इज्यो महेज्यश्च क्रतुः सत्रं सतां गतिः । सर्वदर्शी विमुक्तात्मा सर्वज्ञो ज्ञानमुत्तमम् ॥
lines.iast: yajña ijyo mahejyaś ca kratuḥ satraṃ satāṃ gatiḥ | sarva-darśī vimuktātmā sarva-jño jñānam uttamam ||
lines.english_literal: Sacrifice, the-worshipped, greatly-worshipped, the-rite, the-session, refuge-of-the-good; all-seeing, liberated-Self, all-knowing, supreme-knowledge.
lines.english_poetic: He is the Yajña; the one to be worshipped in sacrifices; alone giving the blessing of liberation; the Kratu (sacrifice with a post); the Satra (session); refuge of the holy. All-seeing by inborn insight; naturally liberated; all-knowing; knowledge supreme, beyond time and space.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 48 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [449] of the nature of ordained dharma

lines.devanagari: सुव्रतः सुमुखः सूक्ष्मः सुघोषः सुखदः सुहृत् । मनोहरो जितक्रोधो वीरबाहुर्विदारणः ॥
lines.iast: su-vrataḥ su-mukhaḥ sūkṣmaḥ su-ghoṣaḥ sukha-daḥ su-hṛt | mano-haro jita-krodho vīra-bāhur vidāraṇaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Of-good-vow, fair-faced, subtle, of-good-sound, joy-giver, true-friend; mind-stealer, anger-conqueror, hero-armed, the-render.
lines.english_poetic: Magnanimous vow-keeper, savior of all refuge-seekers; pleasant-faced; subtle (without gross causes like sound); of melodious Veda-sound; bestower of happiness; the true friend (Suhṛt — Gītā 5.29). Stealer-of-the-mind by His blissful nature; conqueror of anger; whose arms are capable of heroic deeds (slaying Asuras); render of those contrary to dharma.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 49 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [460] true friend who helps without return
[461] stealer of the mind by His bliss

lines.devanagari: स्वापनः स्ववशो व्यापी नैकात्मा नैककर्मकृत् । वत्सरो वत्सलो वत्सी रत्नगर्भो धनेश्वरः ॥
lines.iast: svāpanaḥ sva-vaśo vyāpī naika-ātmā naika-karma-kṛt | vatsaro vatsalo vatsī ratna-garbho dhaneśvaraḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The-sleep-bringer, self-controlled, all-pervading, of-many-selves, of-many-acts; the-year, affectionate, calf-protector, jewel-wombed, wealth-Lord.
lines.english_poetic: Who enfolds jīvas in the sleep of ajñāna; dominated only by oneself; interpenetrating like ākāśa; manifest in many forms as subsidiary agencies; engaging in innumerable creation-acts. The Year-itself in whom everything dwells; affectionate as a mother for her calf; protector of the dear ones; whose womb holds all jewels (the ocean); Lord of all wealth.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 50 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [465] enfolds jīvas in sleep of ajñāna

lines.devanagari: धर्मगुब्धर्मकृद्धर्मी सदसत्क्षरमक्षरम् । अविज्ञाता सहस्रांशुर्विधाता कृतलक्षणः ॥
lines.iast: dharma-gub dharma-kṛd dharmī sad-asat kṣaram akṣaram | avijñātā sahasrāṃśur vidhātā kṛta-lakṣaṇaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Dharma-guard, dharma-doer, the-dharmin, existent-non-existent, perishable-imperishable; non-knower, thousand-rayed, disposer, marked.
lines.english_poetic: Guardian of dharma; performer of dharma; very embodiment of dharma; both being and non-being, perishable and imperishable. Without the limited jīva-attributes ("avijñātā"); the thousand-rayed sun; unique support of Ananta and other world-bearers; of the nature of consciousness.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 51 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [478] Brahman of the nature of truth

lines.devanagari: गभस्तिनेमिः सत्त्वस्थः सिंहो भूतमहेश्वरः । आदिदेवो महादेवो देवेशो देवभृद्गुरुः ॥
lines.iast: gabhasti-nemiḥ sattva-sthaḥ siṃho bhūta-maheśvaraḥ | ādi-devo mahā-devo deveśo deva-bhṛd guruḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Ray-rim, sattva-abiding, lion, supreme-Lord-of-beings; primordial-god, great-god, lord-of-gods, controller-of-Indra.
lines.english_poetic: Dwelling in the middle of rays as the Sun; specially residing in sattva-guṇa (luminous by nature); irresistible as a lion; supreme Lord of all beings. First of all beings; great by His own supreme self-knowledge; Lord of all gods; even Indra-as-deva-supporter is His subordinate.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 52 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: उत्तरो गोपतिर्गोप्ता ज्ञानगम्यः पुरातनः । शरीरभूतभृद्भोक्ता कपीन्द्रो भूरिदक्षिणः ॥
lines.iast: uttaro go-patir goptā jñāna-gamyaḥ purātanaḥ | śarīra-bhūta-bhṛd bhoktā kapīndro bhūri-dakṣiṇaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The-uplifted, cow-Lord, protector, knowledge-knowable, ancient; body-element-bearer, enjoyer, monkey-Lord, of-many-gifts.
lines.english_poetic: Liberated from saṃsāra; Lord of the cows (as Gopāla) / earth; protector of all beings; knowable only through knowledge; not limited by time. Master of the five elements (of which the body is made); enjoyer of infinite bliss; Indra-of-the-monkeys (Rāma's Vibhīṣaṇa-affined incarnation); receiver of many dakṣiṇas in yajñas.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 53 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: सोमपोऽमृतपः सोमः पुरुजित्पुरुसत्तमः । विनयो जयः सत्यसन्धो दाशार्हः सात्वताम्पतिः ॥
lines.iast: soma-po 'mṛta-paḥ somaḥ pu-rujit puru-sattamaḥ | vinayo jayaḥ satya-sandho dāśārhaḥ sātvatāṃ-patiḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Soma-drinker, nectar-drinker, the-Moon, victor-of-many, best-among-many; humility, victory, of-true-resolve, fit-for-gifts, Lord-of-Sātvatas.
lines.english_poetic: Drinker of Soma as the Devatā in every yajña; drinker of His own immortal bliss; the moon who invigorates plants; victor over many; best among the many (Puruṣottama as both 'puru' great and 'sattama' best). Humility itself (or punisher of the wicked); victorious; of true resolve; deserving of charitable offerings; Lord of the Sātvata clan.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 54 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [503] drinker of Soma in every yajña
[504] drinker of His own immortal bliss

lines.devanagari: जीवो विनयिता साक्षी मुकुन्दोऽमितविक्रमः । अम्भोनिधिरनन्तात्मा महोदधिशयोऽन्तकः ॥
lines.iast: jīvo vinayitā sākṣī mukundo 'mita-vikramaḥ | ambho-nidhir anantātmā maho-dadhi-śayo 'ntakaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Life, controller, witness, mokṣa-giver, of-immeasurable-stride; ocean, infinite-Self, reclining-on-great-ocean, ender.
lines.english_poetic: As Kṣetrajña associated with prāṇas (the jīva); witness of devotees' worshipful attitude; bestower of mokṣa (Mukunda); whose three strides as Vāmana were limitless. The ocean in whom all beings (Ambas) dwell; whose Self is undetermined by space-time-causation; reclining on the cosmic ocean at dissolution; ender of all beings.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 55 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [520] reclining on the great cosmic ocean

lines.devanagari: अजो महार्हः स्वाभाव्यो जितामित्रः प्रमोदनः । आनन्दो नन्दनो नन्दः सत्यधर्मा त्रिविक्रमः ॥
lines.iast: ajo mahārhaḥ svābhāvyo jitāmitraḥ pramodanaḥ | ānando nandano nandaḥ satya-dharmā tri-vikramaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Unborn-Kāma, greatly-worthy, naturally-perfect, foe-conqueror, ever-joyous; bliss, joy-giver, joyful, of-true-dharma, three-strider.
lines.english_poetic: 'A' indicates Mahāviṣṇu; born of Him as Kāmadeva ("Ajaḥ"); fit for worship; eternally perfect, naturally beginningless; conqueror of inner foes (attachment, anger) and outer ones (Rāvaṇa, Kumbhakarṇa); ever joyous in His own bliss. Bliss itself; giver of delight; of all perfections; whose dharma is truth (and whose knowledge is true); the three-strider (Vāmana).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 56 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: महर्षिः कपिलाचार्यः कृतज्ञो मेदिनीपतिः । त्रिपदस्त्रिदशाध्यक्षो महाश‍ृङ्गः कृतान्तकृत् ॥
lines.iast: maharṣiḥ kapilācāryaḥ kṛta-jño medinī-patiḥ | tri-padas tri-daśādhyakṣo mahā-śṛṅgaḥ kṛtānta-kṛt ||
lines.english_literal: Great-seer, teacher-Kapila, knower-of-the-done, earth-Lord; three-strided, witness-of-three-states, great-antenna, end-maker.
lines.english_poetic: Great seer as Kapila, master of all Vedas (Kapila taught Sāṅkhya); knower of the world-as-effect; Lord of the earth; of three strides (as Trivikrama or as the three Vyāhṛtis Bhū-Bhuvaḥ-Svaḥ); witness of waking-dream-sleep; great-horned (the Matsya-incarnation with great antenna); destroyer of the manifested world at dissolution.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 57 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: महावराहो गोविन्दः सुषेणः कनकाङ्गदी । गुह्यो गभीरो गहनो गुप्तश्चक्रगदाधरः ॥
lines.iast: mahā-varāho govindaḥ suṣeṇaḥ kanakāṅgadī | guhyo gabhīro gahano guptaś cakra-gadā-dharaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Great-boar, Govinda, of-noble-army, gold-armletted; secret, profound, unfathomable, hidden, discus-mace-bearer.
lines.english_poetic: The great cosmic Boar (Varāha) who lifted the earth; known through the Vedic words (Govinda); with armed guard of eternal associates (Suṣeṇa); wearing armlets of gold. Known by esoteric Upaniṣadic teaching; profound in omniscience and majesty; entered only with great difficulty; hidden from words; bearer of the Sudarśana cakra and Kaumodakī gadā.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 58 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: वेधाः स्वाङ्गोऽजितः कृष्णो दृढः सङ्कर्षणोऽच्युतः । वरुणो वारुणो वृक्षः पुष्कराक्षो महामनाः ॥
lines.iast: vedhāḥ svāṅgo 'jitaḥ kṛṣṇo dṛḍhaḥ saṅkarṣaṇo 'cyutaḥ | varuṇo vāruṇo vṛkṣaḥ puṣkarākṣo mahā-manāḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Disposer, self-limbed, unconquered, dark-Kṛṣṇa, firm, attractor-infallible, Varuṇa, Vāruṇa, tree, lotus-eyed, great-minded.
lines.english_poetic: Doer of cosmic regulation; oneself participating in works; never conquered in incarnations; Kṛṣṇa-Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa) / dark-coloured; firm in capacity; the attractor-of-all-at-dissolution who is also the unfallen (Saṅkarṣaṇa-Acyuta as one name). Varuṇa (setting sun) / Vāruṇa (Vasiṣṭha, Agastya); unshakable as a tree; lotus-eyed (or pervader of heart-lotus); great-minded fulfiller of creation-sustenance-dissolution by mind alone.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 59 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: भगवान् भगहाऽऽनन्दी वनमाली हलायुधः । आदित्यो ज्योतिरादित्यः सहिष्णुर्गतिसत्तमः ॥
lines.iast: bhagavān bhaga-hā nandī vana-mālī halāyudhaḥ | ādityo jyotir-ādityaḥ sahiṣṇur gati-sattamaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Blessed-Lord, withdrawer-of-glories, joyful, forest-garlanded, plough-weaponed; born-of-Aditi, light-Aditya, forbearer, supreme-goal.
lines.english_poetic: The Blessed One who knows origin, dissolution, bondage, salvation, knowledge, ignorance — Bhagavān; at dissolution He withdraws the six Bhagas into Himself; whose nature is bliss (Nandī); wearing the wild-forest garland Vaijayantī (woven of the five elements); in Balabhadra-form bearing the plough. Born of Aditi as Vāmana; dwelling in the sun's orb (Jyotir-Āditya); forbearer of heat-cold; supreme goal of all beings.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 60 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [560] withdrawer of the six glories at dissolution
[567] supreme goal and support of all

lines.devanagari: सुधन्वा खण्डपरशुर्दारुणो द्रविणप्रदः । दिवस्पृक् सर्वदृग्व्यासो वाचस्पतिरयोनिजः ॥
lines.iast: su-dhanvā khaṇḍa-paraśur dāruṇo draviṇa-pradaḥ | divaḥ-spṛk sarva-dṛg-vyāso vācas-patir a-yoni-jaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Of-good-bow, axe-of-fragments, harsh, wealth-bestower; sky-touching, all-seeing-Vyāsa, Lord-of-speech, womb-less.
lines.english_poetic: Holder of the excellent bow Śārṅga; battle-axe that destroys enemies; harsh and merciless to those on the evil path; bestower of desired wealth. Touching the heavens; comprehending all (the seer Vyāsa, who arranged the Vedas); master of all learning; not born from a womb (one name with attributive qualification).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 61 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [568] holder of the excellent Śārṅga bow
[570] harsh to those on evil path

lines.devanagari: त्रिसामा सामगः साम निर्वाणं भेषजं भिषक् । संन्यासकृच्छमः शान्तो निष्ठा शान्तिः परायणम् ॥
lines.iast: tri-sāmā sāma-gaḥ sāma nirvāṇaṃ bheṣajaṃ bhiṣak | sannyāsa-kṛc chamaḥ śānto niṣṭhā śāntiḥ parāyaṇam ||
lines.english_literal: Three-Sāmas, Sāma-chanter, the-Sāma, Nirvāṇa, medicine, physician; sannyāsa-instituter, calm, peaceful, abeyance, peace, supreme-refuge.
lines.english_poetic: Praised by the three Devavrata Sāmas; chanter of Sāma-gāna; "Among the Vedas I am the Sāma" (Gītā 10.22). Nirvāṇa (where all misery ceases); the medicine for saṃsāra; the physician. Instituter of the fourth āśrama Sannyāsa for mokṣa; ordainer of inner stillness as the supreme discipline; peaceful (uninterested in pleasures); the abeyance-ground at pralaya; peace; the supreme refuge (parāyaṇa) from which there is no return.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 62 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: शुभाङ्गः शान्तिदः स्रष्टा कुमुदः कुवलेशयः । गोहितो गोपतिर्गोप्ता वृषभाक्षो वृषप्रियः ॥
lines.iast: śubhāṅgaḥ śānti-daḥ sraṣṭā kumudaḥ kuvaleśayaḥ | go-hito go-patir goptā vṛṣabhākṣo vṛṣa-priyaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Beautiful-formed, peace-giver, creator, earth-delighter, water-recliner; cow-benefiter, cow-Lord, protector, dharma-eyed, dharma-beloved.
lines.english_poetic: Of handsome form; giver of śānti (freedom from attachment-aversion); creator at the start of a cycle. Earth-delighter (Ku-muda) by freeing it of burdens; recliner on water / on Ādiśeṣa. Benefiter of cows (Govardhana-lifter); Lord of earth; protector / hider by Māyā; whose eyes (or actions) shower dharma; to whom dharma is dear.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 63 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: अनिवर्ती निवृत्तात्मा सङ्क्षेप्ता क्षेमकृच्छिवः । श्रीवत्सवक्षाः श्रीवासः श्रीपतिः श्रीमतांवरः ॥
lines.iast: anivartī nivṛttātmā saṅkṣeptā kṣema-kṛc chivaḥ | śrīvatsa-vakṣāḥ śrī-vāsaḥ śrī-patiḥ śrīmatāṃ-varaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Never-retreating, withdrawn-Self, the-contractor, welfare-doer, auspicious; Śrīvatsa-chested, abode-of-Śrī, Lord-of-Śrī, supreme-of-the-glorious.
lines.english_poetic: Never retreating in the dharma-battle; whose mind is naturally withdrawn from sense-objects; contractor of the expansive universe at dissolution; doer of welfare (kṣema) to refuge-seekers; auspicious; purifier by the mere utterance of His name. Marked on chest with the Śrīvatsa; on whose chest Śrī Devī dwells; chosen as consort by Lakṣmī at the churning; supreme over Brahmā and other glorious deities.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 64 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: श्रीदः श्रीशः श्रीनिवासः श्रीनिधिः श्रीविभावनः । श्रीधरः श्रीकरः श्रेयः श्रीमाँल्लोकत्रयाश्रयः ॥
lines.iast: śrī-daḥ śrī-śaḥ śrī-nivāsaḥ śrī-nidhiḥ śrī-vibhāvanaḥ | śrī-dharaḥ śrī-karaḥ śreyaḥ śrīmān loka-trayāśrayaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Wealth-giver, Lakṣmī-Lord, Śrī-abode, Śrī-treasury, Śrī-manifester; Śrī-holder, Śrī-maker, the-good, glorious, refuge-of-three-worlds.
lines.english_poetic: Bestower of prosperity; Lord of Goddess Śrī; whose dwelling is in men endowed with virtue and power; the seat of all virtue and power; manifester of every form of prosperity according to karma. Bearer of Śrī (mother of all) on His chest; maker of devotees into virtuous powerful beings; the unfading good (Śreyas); glorious in every way; refuge of all three worlds.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 65 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [609] seat of all virtue and power
[611] bearer of Śrī on the chest
[612] maker of devotees virtuous and powerful

lines.devanagari: स्वक्षः स्वङ्गः शतानन्दो नन्दिर्ज्योतिर्गणेश्वरः । विजितात्माऽविधेयात्मा सत्कीर्तिश्छिन्नसंशयः ॥
lines.iast: svakṣaḥ svaṅgaḥ śatānando nandir jyotir-gaṇeśvaraḥ | vijitātmā vidheyātmā sat-kīrtiś chinna-saṃśayaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Beautiful-eyed, beautiful-limbed, of-hundred-joys, joyful, Lord-of-stars; conqueror-of-self, indeterminable-self, of-true-fame, severed-doubt.
lines.english_poetic: Whose eyes are handsome like lotus flowers; whose limbs are beautiful; non-dual in nature, of supreme bliss; the joyful one; Lord of the stars (jyotir-gaṇa). Conqueror of the mind; whose form cannot be defined as 'only this'; whose fame is real and unfading; whose knowledge is so clear that no doubts exist.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 66 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: उदीर्णः सर्वतश्चक्षुरनीशः शाश्वतस्थिरः । भूशयो भूषणो भूतिर्विशोकः शोकनाशनः ॥
lines.iast: udīrṇaḥ sarvataś-cakṣur anīśaḥ śāśvata-sthiraḥ | bhū-śayo bhūṣaṇo bhūtir viśokaḥ śoka-nāśanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Superior, eyes-on-all-sides, masterless, eternally-firm; earth-sleeper, the-ornament, glory, sorrow-less, sorrow-destroyer.
lines.english_poetic: Superior to all beings; with eyes everywhere (pure consciousness sees all directions); none can be His master; eternal yet ever the same. Lay on the earth at Laṅkā's seashore (Rāma seeking the way); adorned the earth by His incarnations; the very essence and glorious source of all; without sorrow, being bliss; effacer of devotees' sorrows by mere remembrance.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 67 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [629] lay on the earth (Rāma at Laṅkā's shore)

lines.devanagari: अर्चिष्मानर्चितः कुम्भो विशुद्धात्मा विशोधनः । अनिरुद्धोऽप्रतिरथः प्रद्युम्नोऽमितविक्रमः ॥
lines.iast: arciṣmān arcitaḥ kumbho viśuddhātmā viśodhanaḥ | aniruddho 'pratirathaḥ pradyumno 'mita-vikramaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Radiant, the-worshipped, pot, pure-Self, purifier; unobstructed, no-rival-chariot, Pradyumna, of-immeasurable-stride.
lines.english_poetic: By whose rays sun, moon, fire are endowed with light; worshipped by Brahmā and other deva-objects of worship; the pot in which everything is contained; pure Self beyond the three guṇas; purifier of all sins by mere remembrance. Aniruddha (last of the four Vyūhas — Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa, Pradyumna, Aniruddha); without an equal antagonist; Pradyumna of unique wealth (or the third Vyūha); of immeasurable prowess.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 68 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [634] giver of light to sun, moon, fire
[638] purifier of all sins by remembrance

lines.devanagari: कालनेमिनिहा वीरः शौरिः शूरजनेश्वरः । त्रिलोकात्मा त्रिलोकेशः केशवः केशिहा हरिः ॥
lines.iast: kāla-nemi-nihā vīraḥ śauriḥ śūra-janeśvaraḥ | tri-lokātmā tri-lokeśaḥ keśavaḥ keśi-hā hariḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Slayer-of-Kālanemi, hero, son-of-Sura, Lord-of-heroes; three-world-Self, Lord-of-three-worlds, beautiful-haired, Keśi-slayer, Hari.
lines.english_poetic: Destroyer of the Asura Kālanemi; the courageous; born in Sura's clan as Kṛṣṇa; controller even of Indra and other great powers. The Self of all three worlds; their Lord; Keśava (with beautiful locks / radiant rays); slayer of the Asura Keśin; Hari, who destroys saṃsāra and the ignorance that causes it.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 69 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [645] born of Sura's clan as Kṛṣṇa

lines.devanagari: कामदेवः कामपालः कामी कान्तः कृतागमः । अनिर्देश्यवपुर्विष्णुर्वीरोऽनन्तो धनञ्जयः ॥
lines.iast: kāma-devaḥ kāma-pālaḥ kāmī kāntaḥ kṛtāgamaḥ | anirdeśya-vapur viṣṇur vīro 'nanto dhanañjayaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Desire-god, desire-protector, the-desiring, beloved, made-the-scriptures; indescribable-form, Viṣṇu, hero, infinite, Arjuna-as-Vibhūti.
lines.english_poetic: Desired by all seeking the four life-goals; protector / fulfiller of their desires; by nature all-fulfilled; supremely beautiful (or ender of Brahmā's lifetime); creator of all scriptures (śruti, smṛti, āgama). His form cannot be pointed out (being above guṇas); Viṣṇu whose brilliance spans sky and earth; the heroic mover; infinite, beyond space-time-being; Dhanañjaya — Arjuna (His Vibhūti who conquered the four quarters and acquired great wealth).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 70 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [652] desired by seekers of four goals
[657] form unable to be pointed out

lines.devanagari: ब्रह्मण्यो ब्रह्मकृद् ब्रह्मा ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविवर्धनः । ब्रह्मविद् ब्राह्मणो ब्रह्मी ब्रह्मज्ञो ब्राह्मणप्रियः ॥
lines.iast: brahmaṇyo brahma-kṛd brahmā brahma brahma-vivardhanaḥ | brahma-vid brāhmaṇo brahmī brahma-jño brāhmaṇa-priyaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Brahma-promoter, Brahma-doer, the-Creator, Brahman, Brahma-augmenter; Veda-knower, the-Brahmin, Brahmā-rooted, Brahma-knower, Brahmin-beloved.
lines.english_poetic: Promoter of the Veda, the Brāhmaṇas, and knowledge; performer of Brahma (tapas); the Creator-Brahmā; the Absolute (Truth-Knowledge-Infinity); augmenter of austerity. Knower of the Vedas and their meaning; the Brāhmaṇa who instructs the world; in whom tapas, Veda, mind, prāṇa are established; knower of Brahman's nature; beloved of holy ones.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 71 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [668] the Brāhmaṇa who instructs the world
[669] in whom tapas, Veda, prāṇa abide

lines.devanagari: महाक्रमो महाकर्मा महातेजा महोरगः । महाक्रतुर्महायज्वा महायज्ञो महाहविः ॥
lines.iast: mahā-kramo mahā-karmā mahā-tejā mahoragaḥ | mahā-kratur mahā-yajvā mahā-yajño mahā-haviḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Great-stride, great-act, great-brilliance, great-serpent; great-rite, great-sacrificer, great-sacrifice, great-oblation.
lines.english_poetic: With enormous strides (Vāmana); performing great works like world-creation; whose brilliance illumines sun and stars; the great serpent (Ananta-form). The great Kratu (sacrifice with post); great performer of yajñas for world's good; the great Yajña itself; the great Havis — "the universe offered as oblation into the fire of the Self that is Brahman."
lines.explanation: **Śloka 72 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: स्तव्यः स्तवप्रियः स्तोत्रं स्तुतिः स्तोता रणप्रियः । पूर्णः पूरयिता पुण्यः पुण्यकीर्तिरनामयः ॥
lines.iast: stavyaḥ stava-priyaḥ stotraṃ stutiḥ stotā raṇa-priyaḥ | pūrṇaḥ pūrayitā puṇyaḥ puṇya-kīrtir anāmayaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The-praised, hymn-lover, the-hymn, praise, the-praiser, battle-lover; full, the-filler, holy, of-holy-fame, disease-less.
lines.english_poetic: Object of all laudation, never praising another; pleased with hymns; the hymn (stotra) itself; the act of praise (stuti); also the praiser (being all-forms); fond of battle for protecting the world (sporting his five weapons). Self-fulfilled; bestower of all fulfilment on others; holy by mere hearing; of fame that confers merit; untouched by any disease.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 73 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [688] holy by mere hearing of Him

lines.devanagari: मनोजवस्तीर्थकरो वसुरेता वसुप्रदः । वसुप्रदो वासुदेवो वसुर्वसुमना हविः ॥
lines.iast: mano-javas tīrtha-karo vasu-retā vasu-pradaḥ | vasu-prado vāsudevo vasur vasu-manā haviḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Mind-swift, ford-maker, gold-seed, wealth-giver; wealth-giver, Vāsudeva, wealth, generous-minded, oblation.
lines.english_poetic: Swift as the mind (being all-pervading); maker of fords (tīrtha = vidyā); whose seed (retas) is gold (Vasu); gladly bestowing wealth in abundance. Bestower of the highest wealth (mokṣa); son of Vasudeva; in whom all creation dwells; whose mind dwells equally in all; the very oblation (havis).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 74 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: सद्गतिः सत्कृतिः सत्ता सद्भूतिः सत्परायणः । शूरसेनो यदुश्रेष्ठः सन्निवासः सुयामुनः ॥
lines.iast: sad-gatiḥ sat-kṛtiḥ sattā sad-bhūtiḥ sat-parāyaṇaḥ | śūra-seno yadu-śreṣṭhaḥ san-nivāsaḥ su-yāmunaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Goal-of-the-good, of-good-acts, pure-being, of-true-glory, supreme-refuge-of-the-good; of-heroic-army, best-of-Yadus, abode-of-the-good, of-fine-Yamunā-folk.
lines.english_poetic: Goal of the holy; whose acts protect the world; pure-being (without internal or external differences); the Paramātman of pure existence-consciousness manifesting variously; the supreme status of those who have realized Truth. Having an army of heroes like Hanumān; greatest of the Yadus; resort of holy knowers; surrounded by Yamunā-folk (Devakī, Vasudeva, Yaśodā, Nanda, Balabhadra, Subhadrā).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 75 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: भूतावासो वासुदेवः सर्वासुनिलयोऽनलः । दर्पहा दर्पदो दृप्तो दुर्धरोऽथापराजितः ॥
lines.iast: bhūtāvāso vāsudevaḥ sarvāsu-nilayo 'nalaḥ | darpa-hā darpa-do dṛpto durdharo 'thāparājitaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Beings-abode, Vāsudeva, all-prāṇa-resort, limitless; pride-destroyer, pride-giver, content, hard-to-hold, unconquered.
lines.english_poetic: In whom all beings dwell; Vāsudeva, the Divinity who covers the universe by Māyā; resort of all prāṇas of all beings; whose wealth/power has no limit. Destroyer of the pride of the unrighteous; granter of self-respect to the righteous; ever satisfied by His own bliss; very difficult to be held in meditation; never conquered by inner enemies or outer Asuras.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 76 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [716] very difficult to hold in meditation

lines.devanagari: विश्वमूर्तिर्महामूर्तिर्दीप्तमूर्तिरमूर्तिमान् । अनेकमूर्तिरव्यक्तः शतमूर्तिः शताननः ॥
lines.iast: viśva-mūrtir mahā-mūrtir dīpta-mūrtir a-mūrtimān | aneka-mūrtir a-vyaktaḥ śata-mūrtiḥ śatānanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: World-formed, great-formed, radiant-formed, body-less; many-formed, unmanifest, hundred-formed, hundred-faced.
lines.english_poetic: Whose body is the universe (being its soul); of enormous form on Ādiśeṣa-bedstead; of radiant knowledge-form; without a body born of karma. Assuming many bodies in incarnations as it pleases Him for the world's help; yet unable to be described as 'this'; of a hundred (= many) forms; of a hundred faces.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 77 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: एको नैकः सवः कः किं यत् तत्पदमनुत्तमम् । लोकबन्धुर्लोकनाथो माधवो भक्तवत्सलः ॥
lines.iast: eko naikaḥ savaḥ kaḥ kiṃ yat tat padam anuttamam | loka-bandhur loka-nātho mādhavo bhakta-vatsalaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: One, not-one, Soma-yajña, joy, what?, that-which, that, status-supreme; world-friend, world-Lord, Mādhava, devotee-affectionate.
lines.english_poetic: One without differences; many bodies through Māyā; the Soma-yajña; pure joy (Ka); fit-to-be-contemplated (Kim); the self-subsisting (Yat); the expanding (Tat); the supreme Status beyond which there is nothing. Friend of the world; Lord prayed to by all worlds; born in Madhu's clan; love for His devotees.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 78 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [726] one, without any kind of difference
[735] Lord prayed to by all worlds

lines.devanagari: सुवर्णवर्णो हेमाङ्गो वराङ्गश्चन्दनाङ्गदी । वीरहा विषमः शून्यो घृताशीरचलश्चलः ॥
lines.iast: su-varṇa-varṇo hemāṅgo varāṅgaś candanāṅgadī | vīra-hā viṣamaḥ śūnyo ghṛtāśīr acalaś-calaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Gold-coloured, gold-limbed, of-noble-form, sandal-armletted; hero-slayer, peerless, the-empty, of-blessing, unmoving-moving.
lines.english_poetic: Of golden hue; with golden limbs; noble of form; adorned with sandal-paste armlets. Destroyer of demon-heroes (Hiraṇyakaśipu); peerless; appearing as 'empty' (being without sense-attributes); of unfailing blessings; never moving from His true nature; yet moving in the form of air.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 79 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: अमानी मानदो मान्यो लोकस्वामी त्रिलोकधृक् । सुमेधा मेधजो धन्यः सत्यमेधा धराधरः ॥
lines.iast: a-mānī māna-do mānyo loka-svāmī tri-loka-dhṛt | su-medhā medha-jo dhanyaḥ satya-medhā dharā-dharaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Mānī-less, honour-giver, honoured, world-master, three-world-bearer; of-fine-wisdom, born-of-sacrifice, blessed, of-true-wisdom, earth-bearer.
lines.english_poetic: Pure Consciousness without identification with the non-self; bestower of mind/regard in non-self by Māyā (or destroyer of false self-sense in the wise); to be adored by all; Lord of the fourteen worlds; supporter of the three worlds. Of beneficent intelligence; arose from the great yajña; ever self-satisfied; whose intelligence is fruitful; supporter of the worlds in His Ananta-portions.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 80 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [749] giver of regard / destroyer of false self-sense

lines.devanagari: तेजोवृषो द्युतिधरः सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः । प्रग्रहो निग्रहो व्यग्रो नैकश‍ृङ्गो गदाग्रजः ॥
lines.iast: tejo-vṛṣo dyuti-dharaḥ sarva-śastra-bhṛtāṃ varaḥ | pragraho nigraho vyagro naika-śṛṅgo gadāgrajaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Brilliance-rainer, brilliance-holder, foremost-of-weapon-bearers; gracious-receiver, controller, attentive, many-horned, Mantra-firstborn.
lines.english_poetic: As the sun, He rains continually; holder of perpetual brilliance; superior to all weapon-bearers. Accepts devotees' offerings with delight; controls and destroys all; intensely attentive in granting prayers; with many horns (as Cosmic Bull); revealed first by Mantra (Nigada) / elder brother of Gada.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 81 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: चतुर्मूर्तिश्चतुर्बाहुश्चतुर्व्यूहश्चतुर्गतिः । चतुरात्मा चतुर्भावश्चतुर्वेदविदेकपात् ॥
lines.iast: catur-mūrtiś catur-bāhuś catur-vyūhaś catur-gatiḥ | catur-ātmā catur-bhāvaś catur-veda-vid eka-pāt ||
lines.english_literal: Four-formed, four-armed, four-arrayed, four-goaled; four-Selfed, four-natured, four-Veda-knower, one-footed.
lines.english_poetic: Of four aspects (Virāṭ, Sūtra, Avyākṛta, Turīya) / with four horns (white, red, yellow, black); four-armed (Vāsudeva-form); of four Vyūha-manifestations; the goal of the four āśramas and four varṇas. Specially endowed Self (puissant, without attachment); from whom the four puruṣārthas arise; knower of the four Vedas; with a single Pāda (cosmic manifestation).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 82 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [769] goal of four āśramas and four varṇas

lines.devanagari: समावर्तोऽनिवृत्तात्मा दुर्जयो दुरतिक्रमः । दुर्लभो दुर्गमो दुर्गो दुरावासो दुरारिहा ॥
lines.iast: samā-varto 'nivṛttātmā durjayo duratikramaḥ | durlabho durgamo durgo durāvāso durāri-hā ||
lines.english_literal: Whirl-of-saṃsāra, unrestricted-Self, unconquerable, untransgressable; hard-to-attain, hard-to-go, fort, hard-to-dwell, slayer-of-bad-foes.
lines.english_poetic: Whirler of the wheel of saṃsāra; not separated from any place (all-pervading); unconquerable; whose command none can transgress, not even the sun. Attainable only by rare bhakti; difficult to approach; rendered difficult by obstructions (Durga); whom yogis bring to abide in their hearts only with great difficulty (durāvāsa); destroyer of evil foes (Asuras).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 83 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [781] difficult to bring into the heart

lines.devanagari: शुभाङ्गो लोकसारङ्गः सुतन्तुस्तन्तुवर्धनः । इन्द्रकर्मा महाकर्मा कृतकर्मा कृतागमः ॥
lines.iast: śubhāṅgo loka-sāraṅgaḥ su-tantus tantu-vardhanaḥ | indra-karmā mahā-karmā kṛta-karmā kṛtāgamaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Auspicious-formed, world-essence-grasper, fine-thread, thread-augmenter; Indra-acting, great-acting, accomplished-act, scripture-maker.
lines.english_poetic: Whose form is auspicious for meditation; like a honey-beetle (sāraṅga) He grasps the world's essence; the infinitely-extended cosmic web (sutantu); augmenter and contractor of this web. His acts are noble like Indra's; great elements like ākāśa are His works; whose work is fulfilled, nothing more to accomplish; revealer of the Vedic āgama.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 84 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: उद्भवः सुन्दरः सुन्दो रत्ननाभः सुलोचनः । अर्को वाजसनः श‍ृङ्गी जयन्तः सर्वविज्जयी ॥
