lines.iast: udbhavaḥ sundaraḥ sundo ratna-nābhaḥ su-locanaḥ | arko vāja-sanaḥ śṛṅgī jayantaḥ sarva-vij-jayī ||
lines.english_literal: Self-arisen, beautiful, tender, jewel-naveled, beautiful-eyed; the-sun, food-giver, horned, victorious, all-knowing-conqueror.
lines.english_poetic: Assuming noble embodiments by His will; gracefully attractive; of extreme tenderness; beautiful-naveled (ratna = beautiful); brilliant-eyed (knowledge of everything). Worshipped even by Brahmā (Arka); giver of vāja (food) to entreating ones; great-antenna (Matsya); easy conqueror of foes; all-knowing yet also victor over inner attachments and outer Hiraṇyākṣa.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 85 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [797] giver of food to entreating ones

lines.devanagari: सुवर्णबिन्दुरक्षोभ्यः सर्ववागीश्वरेश्वरः । महाह्रदो महागर्तो महाभूतो महानिधिः ॥
lines.iast: suvarṇa-bindur akṣobhyaḥ sarva-vāgīśvareśvaraḥ | mahā-hrado mahā-garto mahā-bhūto mahā-nidhiḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Gold-limbed, undisturbable, supreme-of-speech-lords; great-lake, great-pit, great-being, great-treasury.
lines.english_poetic: Whose limbs are golden in brilliance; never perturbed by passions or Asuras; master even of Brahmā and other speech-masters. A great lake of bliss in which yogis dip themselves; whose Māyā is hard to cross like a great pit; not divided by past-present-future; great treasury in which all great elements rest.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 86 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [802] never perturbed by passions or Asuras
[804] great lake of bliss for yogis
[805] Māyā hard to cross like great pit
[807] great precious treasury of all elements

lines.devanagari: कुमुदः कुन्दरः कुन्दः पर्जन्यः पावनोऽनिलः । अमृताशोऽमृतवपुः सर्वज्ञः सर्वतोमुखः ॥
lines.iast: kumudaḥ kundaraḥ kundaḥ parjanyaḥ pāvano 'nilaḥ | amṛtāśo 'mṛta-vapuḥ sarva-jñaḥ sarvato-mukhaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Earth-delighter, jasmine-blesser, jasmine-limbed, rain-cloud, purifier, sleepless; nectar-eater, deathless-formed, all-knowing, faces-everywhere.
lines.english_poetic: Earth-delighter (frees it of burdens); whose blessings are pure as jasmine; whose limbs are jasmine-beautiful; the cloud (extinguishing three-fold tapas: psychological, material, spiritual / raining all desires); purifying mere-remembrance; ever awake (anila = no-sleep). Consumer of His own immortal bliss; whose form is deathless and undecaying; all-knowing; with faces everywhere.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 87 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: सुलभः सुव्रतः सिद्धः शत्रुजिच्छत्रुतापनः । न्यग्रोधोऽदुम्बरोऽश्वत्थश्चाणूरान्ध्रनिषूदनः ॥
lines.iast: sulabhaḥ suvrataḥ siddhaḥ śatru-jic chatru-tāpanaḥ | nyagrodho 'dumbaro 'śvatthaś cāṇūrāndhra-niṣūdanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Easy-to-attain, of-good-vow, perfected, foe-conqueror, foe-tormentor; downward-grower, beyond-sky, impermanent, slayer-of-Cāṇūra.
lines.english_poetic: Easily attained by simple offerings (leaf, flower, fruit) with bhakti; either enjoyer of pure offerings or a non-enjoyer (mere witness); whose objects are always attained, omnipotent. Conqueror of all evil forces; tormentor of Devas' enemies. The cosmic Nyagrodha (banyan growing downward from above), source of all manifest; Udumbara (above the sky, the supreme cause); Aśvattha (impermanent — the world does not last to next day); slayer of Cāṇūra of the Andhra clan.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 88 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [819] enjoyer of pure offerings / mere witness
[823] cosmic banyan, source of all manifest

lines.devanagari: सहस्रार्चिः सप्तजिह्वः सप्तैधाः सप्तवाहनः । अमूर्तिरनघोऽचिन्त्यो भयकृद्भयनाशनः ॥
lines.iast: sahasrārciḥ sapta-jihvaḥ saptaidhāḥ sapta-vāhanaḥ | a-mūrtir anagho 'cintyo bhaya-kṛd bhaya-nāśanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Thousand-rayed, seven-tongued, seven-flames, seven-mounted; formless, sinless, unthinkable, fear-causer, fear-destroyer.
lines.english_poetic: With innumerable rays; the seven tongues of fire (as Agni); seven forms of brilliance (as fire); with seven horses (as Sūrya). Without sin or sorrow; not determinable by any criteria (Achintya); causer of fear in evil-doers / cutter of all fear-root; destroyer of fear for the virtuous.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 89 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [834] causer of fear in evil / cutter of fear-root

lines.devanagari: अणुर्बृहत्कृशः स्थूलो गुणभृन्निर्गुणो महान् । अधृतः स्वधृतः स्वास्यः प्राग्वंशो वंशवर्धनः ॥
lines.iast: aṇur bṛhat kṛśaḥ sthūlo guṇa-bhṛn nirguṇo mahān | a-dhṛtaḥ sva-dhṛtaḥ svāsyaḥ prāg-vaṃśo vaṃśa-vardhanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Subtle, vast, non-material, gross, guṇa-bearer, guṇa-less, great; unsupported, self-supported, beautiful-faced, primordial-lineage, lineage-augmenter.
lines.english_poetic: Extremely subtle (aṇu); huge (bṛhat); non-material (kṛśa); pervading all as 'huge' (sthūla); support of the three guṇas (sattva-rajas-tamas) by which He creates; yet beyond the guṇas; the great. Unsupported by anything external; self-supported; whose face is beautiful, slightly red within like a lotus interior; primordial lineage (other lineages preceded by others, His descendant — the world-system — is not so); augmenter (or destroyer) of the world-system.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 90 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: भारभृत् कथितो योगी योगीशः सर्वकामदः । आश्रमः श्रमणः क्षामः सुपर्णो वायुवाहनः ॥
lines.iast: bhāra-bhṛt kathito yogī yogīśaḥ sarva-kāma-daḥ | āśramaḥ śramaṇaḥ kṣāmaḥ suparṇo vāyu-vāhanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Burden-bearer, the-praised, the-yogi, Lord-of-yogis, all-desire-giver; the-hermitage, exhauster, beyond-modification, fair-winged, air-mover.
lines.english_poetic: Bears the earth's weight as Ananta; praised by every Veda; the Yogi by knowledge; never shaken from yoga (unlike ordinary yogis); fulfiller of every desire. The resting-hermitage for those wandering in saṃsāra-forest; bringer-of-trouble to those without discrimination; beyond all wasting away of beings; the Tree of saṃsāra has excellent leaves (Vedic chandas); for fear of whom Vāyu carries all.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 91 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [848] bearer of earth's weight as Ananta
[857] for whose fear Vāyu carries all

lines.devanagari: धनुर्धरो धनुर्वेदो दण्डो दमयिता दमः । अपराजितः सर्वसहो नियन्ताऽनियमोऽयमः ॥
lines.iast: dhanur-dharo dhanur-vedo daṇḍo damayitā damaḥ | aparājitaḥ sarva-saho niyantā 'niyamo 'yamaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Bow-bearer, archery-knower, the-rod, the-restrainer, restraint; unconquered, all-bearing, controller, no-law-above-him, deathless.
lines.english_poetic: Wielder of the great bow as Rāma; master of the science of archery; the disciplinary rod among disciplinarians; restrainer (Yama and king); the self-restraint that results from enforcement. Never defeated by enemies; expert in all karmas; appointer of each being to its duty; without any overlord enforcing law on Him; without death.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 92 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [858] wielder of the great bow as Rāma

lines.devanagari: सत्त्ववान् सात्त्विकः सत्यः सत्यधर्मपरायणः । अभिप्रायः प्रियर्होऽर्हः प्रियकृत् प्रीतिवर्धनः ॥
lines.iast: sattvavān sāttvikaḥ satyaḥ satya-dharma-parāyaṇaḥ | abhiprāyaḥ priyārho 'rhaḥ priya-kṛt prīti-vardhanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Of-strength, sattva-rooted, true, true-dharma-refuge; the-goal, worthy-of-love, worthy, joy-doer, joy-augmenter.
lines.english_poetic: Endowed with strengthening qualities (heroism, prowess); essentially established in sattva; truly present in good people; refuge of truth-and-righteousness. The intended goal of puruṣārtha-seekers; the being to whom dear offerings are fit; worthy of all worship-rites; doer of what is dear; augmenter of joys of devotees.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 93 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [873] to whom dear offerings are fit

lines.devanagari: विहायसगतिर्ज्योतिः सुरुचिर्हुतभुग्विभुः । रविर्विरोचनः सूर्यः सविता रविलोचनः ॥
lines.iast: vihāyasa-gatir jyotiḥ su-rucir huta-bhug vibhuḥ | ravir virocanaḥ sūryaḥ savitā ravi-locanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Sky-going, light, of-fine-radiance, oblation-eater, all-pervading; sun, the-shining, Sūrya, Savitar, sun-eyed.
lines.english_poetic: Support of Viṣṇu-pada (the upper region); self-luminous consciousness; of attractive radiance/will; receiver of all sacrificial oblations; dwelling everywhere as master of the three worlds. The sun who absorbs all fluids; shining in many ways; Sūrya from whom brilliance comes; Savitar who brings forth all worlds; with the sun as His eye.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 94 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: अनन्तो हुतभुग्भोक्ता सुखदो नैकजोऽग्रजः । अनिर्विण्णः सदामर्षी लोकाधिष्ठानमद्भुतः ॥
lines.iast: ananto huta-bhug bhoktā sukha-do naika-jo 'gra-jaḥ | a-nirviṇṇaḥ sadā-marṣī lokādhiṣṭhānam adbhutaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Infinite, oblation-eater, enjoyer, joy-giver, many-born, first-born; never-heedless, ever-patient, world-foundation, wonderful.
lines.english_poetic: Eternal, all-pervading, indeterminable by space-time; consumer of fire-sacrifice offerings; enjoyer to whom Prakṛti is the object; bestower of liberation; takes birth again and again for dharma; born before everything as Hiraṇyagarbha. Never heedless (all desires secured, no obstruction); ever patient with good people; the foundation of all three worlds though needing none Himself; the wonderful being.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 95 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: सनात्सनातनतमः कपिलः कपिरव्ययः । स्वस्तिदः स्वस्तिकृत्स्वस्ति स्वस्तिभुक्स्वस्तिदक्षिणः ॥
lines.iast: sanāt sanātanatamaḥ kapilaḥ kapir avyayaḥ | svasti-daḥ svasti-kṛt svasti svasti-bhuk svasti-dakṣiṇaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Ancient, most-ancient, Kapila, the-monkey, imperishable; auspicious-giver, auspicious-doer, auspicious, auspicious-enjoyer, auspicious-bestower.
lines.english_poetic: From eternity (Sanāt = time itself, His manifestation); the most ancient of beings, predating even Brahmā; Kapila — subterranean fire light-red in colour; the sun (Kapi = drinker of water by rays); in whom all worlds dissolve at pralaya. Giver of what is auspicious; doer of what is auspicious; whose form is bliss-auspicious; enjoyer/preserver of devotees' Svasti; augmenter of auspiciousness.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 96 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [900] the sun drinking water by rays
[901] in whom worlds dissolve at pralaya

lines.devanagari: अरौद्रः कुण्डली चक्री विक्रम्यूर्जितशासनः । शब्दातिगः शब्दसहः शिशिरः शर्वरीकरः ॥
lines.iast: araudraḥ kuṇḍalī cakrī vikramy ūrjita-śāsanaḥ | śabdātigaḥ śabda-sahaḥ śiśiraḥ śarvarī-karaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Without-anger, ear-ringed, discus-bearer, valorous, of-sublime-command; beyond-sound, sound-purport, cool, night-maker.
lines.english_poetic: Free from action, attachment and anger (the three Raudras); ear-ringed (in Ananta-form); bearer of the Sudarśana cakra (= the Manas-category) for protection; of valorous strides; whose śruti-smṛti commands are of an extremely sublime nature. Cannot be denoted by sound (no sound-grasps Him); yet the purport of all Vedas; cool — shelter to those burning in the three saṃsāric fires; maker of night for both bound and enlightened (saṃsāra is night for the wise, Self is night for the bound).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 97 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [914] cool shelter from three saṃsāric fires

lines.devanagari: अक्रूरः पेशलो दक्षो दक्षिणः क्षमिणांवरः । विद्वत्तमो वीतभयः पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तनः ॥
lines.iast: akrūraḥ peśalo dakṣo dakṣiṇaḥ kṣamiṇāṃ varaḥ | vidvattamo vīta-bhayaḥ puṇya-śravaṇa-kīrtanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Not-cruel, handsome, capable, gracious, best-of-the-patient; greatest-knower, fear-free, holy-hearing-praising.
lines.english_poetic: Without cruelty; handsome in action-mind-word-body; full-grown and strong (does everything quickly); gracious (same nature); greatest of the patient (more patient than all Yogis). Holder of unsurpassable all-inclusive knowledge; eternally free, fearless of saṃsāric life; to hear of whom and sing of whom is meritorious.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 98 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [923] hearing and singing of Him is meritorious

lines.devanagari: उत्तारणो दुष्कृतिहा पुण्यो दुःस्वप्ननाशनः । वीरहा रक्षणः सन्तो जीवनः पर्यवस्थितः ॥
lines.iast: uttāraṇo duṣkṛti-hā puṇyo duḥsvapna-nāśanaḥ | vīra-hā rakṣaṇaḥ santo jīvanaḥ paryavasthitaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Uplifter, evil-deed-destroyer, holy, bad-dream-destroyer; hero-slayer, protector, the-virtuous, life, pervading.
lines.english_poetic: Takes beings across the saṃsāra-ocean; effaces evil effects of evil actions; bestows holiness on remembrancers; saves from dreams foreboding danger when meditated upon. Frees Jīvas from bondage (slayer of saṃsāric heroes); protects the three worlds (as Sattva-guṇa); the good ones (Santaḥ); supporter of all lives as Prāṇa; remains pervading the universe.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 99 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: अनन्तरूपोऽनन्तश्रीर्जितमन्युर्भयापहः । चतुरश्रो गभीरात्मा विदिशो व्यादिशो दिशः ॥
lines.iast: ananta-rūpo 'nanta-śrīr jita-manyur bhayāpahaḥ | catur-aśro gabhīrātmā vidiśo vyādiśo diśaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Infinite-formed, infinite-glory, anger-conqueror, fear-remover; four-cornered, deep-Self, distributor, instructor, the-direction.
lines.english_poetic: Of infinite forms (dwelling in all-comprehending universe); whose Śrī is infinite; conqueror of anger; remover of saṃsāra-fears. Just (gives the fruits of karma to differing forms); whose Self is unfathomable; distributor of varied fruits by competency; gives Indra and other deities directions for their varied functions; as the Vedas, bestows the fruits of ritual on differing beings.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 100 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [940] giver of directions to Indra etc.

lines.devanagari: अनादिर्भूर्भुवो लक्ष्मीः सुवीरो रुचिराङ्गदः । जननो जनजन्मादिर्भीमो भीमपराक्रमः ॥
lines.iast: anādir bhūr-bhuvo lakṣmīḥ su-vīro rucirāṅgadaḥ | janano jana-janmādir bhīmo bhīma-parākramaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Beginningless, earth-support, fortune, of-many-heroic-ways, of-attractive-armlets; birth-giver, root-of-births, awesome, of-awesome-valor.
lines.english_poetic: Without beginning (ultimate cause of all); supporter even of the earth-supporter; bestower of all that is auspicious to the earth; of many brilliant ways of manifestation; with attractive armlets. Giver of birth to all living beings; root cause of the embodied origin of Jīvas; the awesome (cause of fear in Asuras); whose power and courage in His incarnations cause fear in evil-doers.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 101 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: आधारनिलयोऽधाता पुष्पहासः प्रजागरः । ऊर्ध्वगः सत्पथाचारः प्राणदः प्रणवः पणः ॥
lines.iast: ādhāra-nilayo 'dhātā puṣpa-hāsaḥ prajāgaraḥ | ūrdhva-gaḥ sat-pathācāraḥ prāṇa-daḥ praṇavaḥ paṇaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Foundation-of-supports, self-supporter, flower-laugh, ever-awake; up-going, walker-of-true-path, life-giver, Praṇava, transactor.
lines.english_poetic: Support of even the basic supporting elements (ether, air, fire, water, earth); His own support, needing no other; whose manifestation as the universe resembles the blooming of buds into flowers ("laughter" of flowers); particularly awake, being eternal Awareness. Above everything; follower of the conduct of the good; restorer-of-life (as to Parīkṣit); the Praṇava (Oṃ); bestower of karma-fruits according to deeds (paṇa = transaction).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 102 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [951] support of even basic supporting elements
[956] follower of the conduct of the good

lines.devanagari: प्रमाणं प्राणनिलयः प्राणभृत्प्राणजीवनः । तत्त्वं तत्त्वविदेकात्मा जन्ममृत्युजरातिगः ॥
lines.iast: pramāṇaṃ prāṇa-nilayaḥ prāṇa-bhṛt prāṇa-jīvanaḥ | tattvaṃ tattva-vid ekātmā janma-mṛtyu-jarātigaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Measure, prāṇa-resting-place, prāṇa-bearer, prāṇa-life; the-Reality, knower-of-Reality, one-Self, beyond-birth-death-decay.
lines.english_poetic: Self-certifying (Pure Consciousness as the measure); home/dissolving-ground of the Prāṇas; strengthener of Prāṇa as food; sustainer of human beings by Prāṇa-Apāna and other vāyus. The Reality (Tattvam = Brahman, Amṛta, Satya, Paramārtha); knower of His own true nature; the sole Atman in all beings; subsisting without the six transformations (birth, existence, growth, transformation, decay, death).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 103 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [965] knower of His own true nature

lines.devanagari: भूर्भुवःस्वस्तरुस्तारः सविता प्रपितामहः । यज्ञो यज्ञपतिर्यज्वा यज्ञाङ्गो यज्ञवाहनः ॥
lines.iast: bhūr-bhuvaḥ-svas-tarus tāraḥ savitā prapitāmahaḥ | yajño yajña-patir yajvā yajñāṅgo yajña-vāhanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Bhū-Bhuvaḥ-Svaḥ-tree, ferry, Savitar, great-grandfather; sacrifice, sacrifice-Lord, sacrificer, sacrifice-limb, sacrifice-vehicle.
lines.english_poetic: The three Vyāhṛtis Bhū-Bhuvaḥ-Svaḥ (essence of the Veda) form the tree (Brahman-tree); ferry across the saṃsāric ocean; generator of all worlds; father of Brahmā the creator (and so grandfather of all). The very Yajña; Lord and master of yajñas; performer of yajña; all His limbs identified with parts of yajña (as Cosmic Boar); supporter of yajña yielding various fruits.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 104 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

words.meaning: [971] father of Brahmā, grandfather of all

lines.devanagari: यज्ञभृद् यज्ञकृद् यज्ञी यज्ञभुग् यज्ञसाधनः । यज्ञान्तकृद् यज्ञगुह्यमन्नमन्नाद एव च ॥
lines.iast: yajña-bhṛd yajña-kṛd yajñī yajña-bhug yajña-sādhanaḥ | yajñānta-kṛd yajña-guhyam annam annāda eva ca ||
lines.english_literal: Sacrifice-bearer, sacrifice-doer, the-sacrificer, sacrifice-enjoyer, sacrifice-means; sacrifice-ender, sacrifice-secret, food, food-eater.
lines.english_poetic: Bearer of all yajña; performer of yajña at the cosmic beginning and end; the principal Yajñin; enjoyer/protector of yajña; the means or approach of yajña; ender (or fruit) of yajña; the esoteric Jñāna-yajña — the sacrifice of knowledge that is the secret of all yajñas. He is the food eaten (annam) and also the eater of food (annādaḥ).
lines.explanation: **Śloka 105 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: आत्मयोनिः स्वयञ्जातो वैखानः सामगायनः । देवकीनन्दनः स्रष्टा क्षितीशः पापनाशनः ॥
lines.iast: ātma-yoniḥ svayañ-jāto vaikhānaḥ sāma-gāyanaḥ | devakī-nandanaḥ sraṣṭā kṣitīśaḥ pāpa-nāśanaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Self-source, self-born, the-excavator, Sāma-singer; Devakī-joy, creator, earth-Lord, sin-destroyer.
lines.english_poetic: Source of all (no material cause other than Himself); instrumental cause as well; excavator of the earth (Varāha lifting earth); reciter of the Sāma chants. Son of Devakī (Kṛṣṇa); creator of all worlds; master of earth (Rāma); destroyer of sins of those who adore, meditate, remember, and sing of Him.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 106 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: शङ्खभृन्नन्दकी चक्री शार्ङ्गधन्वा गदाधरः । रथाङ्गपाणिरक्षोभ्यः सर्वप्रहरणायुधः ॥
lines.iast: śaṅkha-bhṛn nandakī cakrī śārṅga-dhanvā gadā-dharaḥ | rathāṅga-pāṇir akṣobhyaḥ sarva-praharaṇāyudhaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Conch-bearer, Nandaka-wielder, discus-bearer, Śārṅga-bow-archer, mace-holder; chariot-wheel-armed, unperturbable, of-all-striking-weapons.
lines.english_poetic: Bearer of the conch Pāñcajanya (Tāmasa-ahaṅkāra-symbol from which five elements are born); wielder of the sword Nandaka (Vidyā, spiritual illumination); bearer of the discus Sudarśana (Rājasa-ahaṅkāra, source of Indriyas); aimer of the Śārṅga bow; bearer of the mace Kaumodakī (the Buddhi-category). With the cakra in hand; unperturbed by anything (He controls all these weapons); but the rule does not limit Him — anything that strikes is His weapon. Hence "all is His armament." 🕉 namaḥ.
lines.explanation: **Śloka 107 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: वनमाली गदी शार्ङ्गी शङ्खी चक्री च नन्दकी । श्रीमान् नारायणो विष्णुर्वासुदेवोऽभिरक्षतु ॥ श्री वासुदेवोऽभिरक्षतु ॐ नम इति ।
lines.iast: vana-mālī gadī śārṅgī śaṅkhī cakrī ca nandakī | śrīmān nārāyaṇo viṣṇur vāsudevo 'bhirakṣatu || śrī vāsudevo 'bhirakṣatu oṃ nama iti ||
lines.english_literal: Forest-garlanded, mace-armed, Śārṅga-bow-armed, conch-armed, discus-armed, Nandaka-sword-armed; glorious Nārāyaṇa Viṣṇu Vāsudeva — may He protect us. (Chanted three times.)
lines.english_poetic: May He protect us — Lord Nārāyaṇa, who wears the wild-forest garland (Vaijayantī), wields the mace (Kaumodakī), the bow (Śārṅga), the conch (Pāñcajanya), the discus (Sudarśana), and the sword (Nandaka); the glorious Viṣṇu, who is Vāsudeva. May Śrī Vāsudeva protect us. Oṃ, salutations. (Recited thrice — the closing benediction of the Sahasranāmam.)
lines.explanation: **Śloka 108 of 108** — names from the canonical Vishnu Sahasranāmam (Mahābhārata, Anuśāsana Parva 149).

lines.devanagari: इतीदं कीर्तनीयस्य केशवस्य महात्मनः । नाम्नां सहस्रं दिव्यानामशेषेण प्रकीर्तितम् ॥
lines.iast: itīdaṃ kīrtanīyasya keśavasya mahātmanaḥ | nāmnāṃ sahasraṃ divyānām aśeṣeṇa prakīrtitam ||
lines.english_literal: Thus, of the praiseworthy Keśava, the great soul, the thousand divine names have been declared in their entirety.
lines.english_poetic: Thus the thousand divine names of Keśava the praiseworthy, the great soul, have been declared in full — without remainder.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 1.** Opens the 'fruits of recitation' section, marking the end of the 108 verses.

lines.devanagari: य इदं शृणुयान्नित्यं यश्चापि परिकीर्तयेत् । नाशुभं प्राप्नुयात् किञ्चित् सोऽमुत्रेह च मानवः ॥
lines.iast: ya idaṃ śṛṇuyān-nityaṃ yaścāpi parikīrtayet | nāśubhaṃ prāpnuyāt kiñcit so 'mutreha ca mānavaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Whoever daily hears this, or also recites it, would obtain no misfortune whatsoever — that person, here or hereafter.
lines.english_poetic: Whoever hears this hymn day after day, or recites it, will never encounter any misfortune — neither here in this life nor in the next.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 2** — the broad promise: protection in both worlds for the daily reciter.

lines.devanagari: वेदान्तगो ब्राह्मणः स्यात् क्षत्रियो विजयी भवेत् । वैश्यो धनसमृद्धः स्याच्छूद्रः सुखमवाप्नुयात् ॥
lines.iast: vedānta-go brāhmaṇaḥ syāt kṣatriyo vijayī bhavet | vaiśyo dhana-samṛddhaḥ syāc chūdraḥ sukham avāpnuyāt ||
lines.english_literal: The Brahmin would attain Vedānta-knowledge; the Kṣatriya would become victorious; the Vaiśya would become wealth-rich; the Śūdra would attain happiness.
lines.english_poetic: The Brahmin attains Vedāntic knowledge; the Kṣatriya becomes a victorious warrior; the Vaiśya gains abundant wealth; the Śūdra obtains lasting happiness.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 3** — varṇa-specific fruits. The promise scales: each social estate gets the boon most aligned with its dharma. Notably, all four are promised inclusion, with no exclusion.

lines.devanagari: धर्मार्थी प्राप्नुयाद्धर्ममर्थार्थी चार्थमाप्नुयात् । कामानवाप्नुयात्कामी प्रजार्थी प्राप्नुयात्प्रजाम् ॥
lines.iast: dharmārthī prāpnuyād-dharmam arthārthī cārtham āpnuyāt | kāmān avāpnuyāt kāmī prajārthī prāpnuyāt prajām ||
lines.english_literal: The dharma-seeker would attain dharma; the wealth-seeker would attain wealth; the pleasure-seeker would obtain pleasures; the progeny-seeker would obtain progeny.
lines.english_poetic: He who seeks dharma attains it; he who seeks wealth secures it; he who seeks pleasure enjoys it; he who desires children is blessed with progeny.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 4** — the four puruṣārthas (life goals). The classical Hindu fourfold scheme — dharma, artha, kāma, mokṣa — is here applied: the Sahasranāmam delivers whichever of the first three you ask for, with mokṣa implicit at the end.

lines.devanagari: भक्तिमान् यः सदोत्थाय शुचिस्तद्गतमानसः । सहस्रं वासुदेवस्य नाम्नामेतत् प्रकीर्तयेत् ॥
lines.iast: bhaktimān yaḥ sadotthāya śucis tad-gata-mānasaḥ | sahasraṃ vāsudevasya nāmnām etat prakīrtayet ||
lines.english_literal: The devotee who, ever-rising, pure, mind-fixed-on-Him, may recite this thousand of Vāsudeva's names —
lines.english_poetic: The devotee who rises early, pure of body, mind absorbed in Him, who recites these thousand names of Vāsudeva —
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 5** — the prescription: early-morning recitation, mind absorbed.

lines.devanagari: यशः प्राप्नोति विपुलं ज्ञातिप्राधान्यमेव च । अचलां श्रियमाप्नोति श्रेयः प्राप्नोत्यनुत्तमम् ॥
lines.iast: yaśaḥ prāpnoti vipulaṃ jñāti-prādhānyam eva ca | acalāṃ śriyam āpnoti śreyaḥ prāpnoty anuttamam ||
lines.english_literal: Obtains wide fame and pre-eminence among kin; obtains unshakeable Śrī; obtains unsurpassed Śreyas.
lines.english_poetic: — attains wide fame, leadership among kin; gains unshakeable fortune (Lakṣmī); reaches the highest spiritual good.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 6** — completes the sentence of verse 5. The result: fame, social standing, prosperity, and (the crowning gift) unsurpassed Śreyas — the highest spiritual good, often a synonym for mokṣa.

lines.devanagari: न भयं क्वचिदाप्नोति वीर्यं तेजश्च विन्दति । भवत्यरोगो द्युतिमान् बलरूपगुणान्वितः ॥
lines.iast: na bhayaṃ kvacid āpnoti vīryaṃ tejaś ca vindati | bhavaty arogo dyutimān bala-rūpa-guṇānvitaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Obtains no fear anywhere; finds valour and radiance; becomes disease-free, lustrous, endowed with strength, beauty, and virtue.
lines.english_poetic: He never encounters fear from any quarter; he finds valour and radiance; he becomes disease-free, lustrous, endowed with strength, beauty, and virtue.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 7** — physical and psychological wellbeing.

lines.devanagari: रोगार्तो मुच्यते रोगाद् बद्धो मुच्येत बन्धनात् । भयान्मुच्येत भीतस्तु मुच्येतापन्न आपदः ॥
lines.iast: rogārto mucyate rogād baddho mucyeta bandhanāt | bhayān mucyeta bhītas tu mucyetāpanna āpadaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The disease-afflicted is freed from disease; the bound is freed from bondage; the frightened is freed from fear; the distressed is freed from distress.
lines.english_poetic: The diseased is cured; the captive is freed; the frightened finds peace; one in distress is delivered.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 8** — the famous four-fold liberation promise. Often inscribed on Viṣṇu temple walls.

lines.devanagari: दुर्गाण्यतितरत्याशु पुरुषः पुरुषोत्तमम् । स्तुवन्नामसहस्रेण नित्यं भक्तिसमन्वितः ॥
lines.iast: durgāṇy atitaraty āśu puruṣaḥ puruṣottamam | stuvan nāma-sahasreṇa nityaṃ bhakti-samanvitaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: A person, praising the Supreme Person daily with the thousand names, full of devotion, swiftly crosses difficulties.
lines.english_poetic: The person who, full of devotion, daily praises the Supreme Person through the thousand names — swiftly crosses every impassable difficulty.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 9** — the 'durga' (difficulty) is overcome by daily devotional recitation.

lines.devanagari: वासुदेवाश्रयो मर्त्यो वासुदेवपरायणः । सर्वपापविशुद्धात्मा याति ब्रह्म सनातनम् ॥
lines.iast: vāsudevāśrayo martyo vāsudeva-parāyaṇaḥ | sarva-pāpa-viśuddhātmā yāti brahma sanātanam ||
lines.english_literal: A mortal who takes refuge in Vāsudeva, whose highest goal is Vāsudeva, purified of all sin in soul, goes to the eternal Brahman.
lines.english_poetic: The mortal who takes refuge in Vāsudeva, whose highest goal is Vāsudeva alone, whose soul is cleansed of all sin — goes to the eternal Brahman.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 10** — the liberation promise. 'Vāsudevāśraya' (refuge in Vāsudeva) + 'parāyaṇa' (highest goal) = sanātana brahma.

lines.devanagari: न वासुदेवभक्तानामशुभं विद्यते क्वचित् । जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिभयं नैवोपजायते ॥
lines.iast: na vāsudeva-bhaktānām aśubhaṃ vidyate kvacit | janma-mṛtyu-jarā-vyādhi-bhayaṃ naivopajāyate ||
lines.english_literal: Of Vāsudeva's devotees, no inauspiciousness exists anywhere; the fear of birth-death-old-age-disease does not even arise.
lines.english_poetic: For the devotees of Vāsudeva, nothing inauspicious ever exists. The fear of birth, death, old age, and disease does not even arise.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 11** — the strongest guarantee: not even the fear of saṃsāra arises in Vāsudeva's devotees.

lines.devanagari: अर्जुन उवाच — पद्मपत्रविशालाक्ष पद्मनाभ सुरोत्तम । भक्तानामनुरक्तानां त्राता भव जनार्दन ॥
lines.iast: arjuna uvāca — padma-patra-viśālākṣa padma-nābha surottama | bhaktānām anuraktānāṃ trātā bhava janārdana ||
lines.english_literal: Arjuna said: O wide-eyed-as-lotus-petals, lotus-naveled, foremost of the celestials — be the protector of devoted, affectionate ones, O Janārdana.
lines.english_poetic: Arjuna said: O lotus-petal-eyed Lord, lotus-naveled, foremost of the celestials — be the eternal protector of Your devoted and affectionate seekers, O Janārdana.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 12** — Arjuna's prayer.

lines.devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच — यो मां नामसहस्रेण स्तोतुमिच्छति पाण्डव । सोऽहमेकेन श्लोकेन स्तुत एव न संशयः ॥
lines.iast: śrī-bhagavān uvāca — yo māṃ nāma-sahasreṇa stotum icchati pāṇḍava | so 'ham ekena ślokena stuta eva na saṃśayaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: The Lord said: He who wishes to praise me with the thousand names, O Pāṇḍava — I am praised by him even with a single verse, no doubt.
lines.english_poetic: The Lord said: O Pāṇḍava! He who desires to praise Me through the thousand names — by a single verse alone, he has praised Me fully. There is no doubt.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 13** — the Lord's reassurance: intent matters more than completeness.

lines.devanagari: व्यास उवाच — वासनाद्वासुदेवस्य वासितं ते जगत्त्रयम् । सर्वभूतनिवासोऽसि वासुदेव नमोऽस्तु ते ॥
lines.iast: vyāsa uvāca — vāsanād vāsudevasya vāsitaṃ te jagat-trayam | sarva-bhūta-nivāso 'si vāsudeva namo 'stu te ||
lines.english_literal: Vyāsa said: By the dwelling of Vāsudeva, the three worlds are inhabited; You are the dwelling of all beings, O Vāsudeva — salutations to You.
lines.english_poetic: Vyāsa said: Because You dwell within all, the three worlds are perfumed by Your presence. You are the abode of all beings — O Vāsudeva, salutations to You.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 14** — Vyāsa's etymology of Vāsudeva: 'one whose dwelling (vāsa) makes the worlds habitable'. A poetic play on the name.

lines.devanagari: पार्वत्युवाच — केनोपायेन लघुना विष्णोर्नामसहस्रकम् । पठ्यते पण्डितैर्नित्यं श्रोतुमिच्छाम्यहं प्रभो ॥
lines.iast: pārvaty uvāca — kenopāyena laghunā viṣṇor nāma-sahasrakam | paṭhyate paṇḍitair nityaṃ śrotum icchāmy ahaṃ prabho ||
lines.english_literal: Pārvatī said: By what brief means is Viṣṇu's thousand-of-names daily recited by the learned? I wish to hear, O Lord.
lines.english_poetic: Pārvatī asked Śiva: By what brief and easy method can the learned daily recite the thousand names of Viṣṇu? I desire to hear this from You, O Lord.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 15** — Pārvatī's question, setting up Śiva's famous reply.

lines.devanagari: ईश्वर उवाच — श्रीराम राम रामेति रमे रामे मनोरमे । सहस्रनाम तत्तुल्यं रामनाम वरानने ॥
lines.iast: īśvara uvāca — śrī-rāma rāma rāmeti rame rāme manorame | sahasra-nāma tat-tulyaṃ rāma-nāma varānane ||
lines.english_literal: Īśvara (Śiva) said: 'Śrī Rāma, Rāma, Rāma' — thus I delight in Rāma, the mind-delighter. That name 'Rāma' is equal to the thousand names, O lovely-faced.
lines.english_poetic: Śiva replied to Pārvatī: 'Śrī Rāma — Rāma — Rāma' — so I rejoice in Rāma, the delight of the mind. That single name 'Rāma' is equal to the entire thousand, O lovely-faced one.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 16** — Śiva's famous reply. The name 'Rāma' alone equals the thousand.

lines.devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाच — नमोऽस्त्वनन्ताय सहस्रमूर्तये सहस्रपादाक्षिशिरोरुबाहवे । सहस्रनाम्ने पुरुषाय शाश्वते सहस्रकोटियुगधारिणे नमः ॥
lines.iast: brahmovāca — namo 'stv anantāya sahasra-mūrtaye sahasra-pādākṣi-śiroru-bāhave | sahasra-nāmne puruṣāya śāśvate sahasra-koṭi-yuga-dhāriṇe namaḥ ||
lines.english_literal: Brahmā said: Salutations to the Infinite, of thousand forms, thousand feet-eyes-heads-thighs-arms. Salutations to the eternal Puruṣa of thousand names, who bears a thousand crore yugas.
lines.english_poetic: Brahmā said: Salutations to the Infinite One, of a thousand forms — thousand-footed, -eyed, -headed, -thighed, -armed. Salutations to the eternal Person of the thousand names, the bearer of a thousand crore yugas.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 17** — Brahmā's salutation echoing the Ṛgvedic Puruṣa Sūkta (10.90).

lines.devanagari: सञ्जय उवाच — यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः । तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम ॥
lines.iast: sañjaya uvāca — yatra yogeśvaraḥ kṛṣṇo yatra pārtho dhanur-dharaḥ | tatra śrīr vijayo bhūtir dhruvā nītir matir mama ||
lines.english_literal: Sañjaya said: Where Yogeśvara Kṛṣṇa is, where Pārtha the bow-bearer is, there is Śrī, victory, abundance, firm policy — my conviction.
lines.english_poetic: Sañjaya said: Where Kṛṣṇa the Lord of Yoga is, where Arjuna the bow-bearer stands — there is prosperity, there is victory, there is abundance, there is firm righteousness. So I am convinced.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 18** — Bhagavad Gītā 18.78 (the closing verse of the entire Gītā).

lines.devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच — अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते । तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ॥ आर्ताः विषण्णाः शिथिलाश्च भीताः घोरेषु च व्याधिषु वर्तमानाः । सङ्कीर्त्य नारायण-शब्द-मात्रं विमुक्त-दुःखाः सुखिनो भवन्तु ॥
lines.iast: śrī-bhagavān uvāca — ananyāś cintayanto māṃ ye janāḥ paryupāsate | teṣāṃ nityābhiyuktānāṃ yoga-kṣemaṃ vahāmy aham || ārtāḥ viṣaṇṇāḥ śithilāś ca bhītāḥ ghoreṣu ca vyādhiṣu vartamānāḥ | saṅkīrtya nārāyaṇa-śabda-mātraṃ vimukta-duḥkhāḥ sukhino bhavantu ||
lines.english_literal: The Lord said: For those people, undistracted, thinking of Me, who worship Me from all sides — for those ever-devoted, I carry yoga-and-kṣema. May those afflicted, distressed, weakened, frightened, dwelling in terrible diseases — by chanting just the word 'Nārāyaṇa' — become released-of-sorrow, become happy.
lines.english_poetic: The Lord said: For those who think only of Me, undistracted, who worship Me from every side — those ever-devoted ones — I personally bear the burden of their acquiring what they need and preserving what they have. Those who are afflicted, distressed, weakened, terrified, suffering from terrible diseases — by chanting the name 'Nārāyaṇa' alone — may they be released from sorrow and become happy.
lines.explanation: **Phala-śruti verse 19** — combines Bhagavad Gītā 9.22 with a powerful closing benediction. 'Saṅkīrtya nārāyaṇa-śabda-mātraṃ' — 'just by chanting the word Nārāyaṇa alone' — is one of the strongest devotional statements in the Mahābhārata.

lines.devanagari: कायेन वाचा मनसेन्द्रियैर्वा बुद्ध्यात्मना वा प्रकृतेः स्वभावात् । करोमि यद्यद् सकलं परस्मै नारायणायेति समर्पयामि ॥
lines.iast: kāyena vācā manasendriyair vā buddhyātmanā vā prakṛteḥ svabhāvāt | karomi yad-yad sakalaṃ parasmai nārāyaṇāyeti samarpayāmi ||
lines.english_literal: Whatever I do by body, speech, mind, senses, intellect, soul, or by the natural disposition of prakṛti — all of it I offer to the Supreme Nārāyaṇa.
lines.english_poetic: Whatever I do — by body, by speech, by mind, by senses, by intellect, by soul, or by the natural flow of my being — I offer all of it to the supreme Nārāyaṇa.
lines.explanation: **Samarpaṇa** — closing surrender. The standard 'end of pūjā' verse, dedicating all action (conscious and instinctive) to Nārāyaṇa.
